review:
Что можно сказать об игре, когда за меня красноречивее скажет время, проведённое в ней? 
Более 1500+ часов куда как более красноречивы. 
Многие могут спросить "что там можно делать столько времени?". Скажу лишь что я проходил её неоднократно, полностью со всему концовками и закрывая все активности, находя пасхалки и изучая местность. Проходил чистую, проходил с различными модами. Проходил "сырую" послерелизную, когда ещё не было оптимизации и была куча багов (благо меня они, как это ни странно не затронули), проходил версии до патча 2.0, и проходил после него. Проходил и на разных уровнях сложности, включая суперхардкор (теперь уже мою основную и любимую).
Это просто одна из любимых моих игр. Да, она далеко не идеальна и, к сожалению не совсем соответствует обещаниям разработчиков огромной кучи возможностей для нас, а так же показанному нам трейлеру. Но всё равно, как по мне, это великая игра, кто бы что ни говорил. Шедевр, и этого слова я не побоюсь!
Часть из обещанных возможностей была допилена в последующих послерелизных патчах, часть допилили в DLC "Призрачная свобода". Часть была сделана или доделана мододелами.

Сеттинг в игре просто потрясающий. Это просто кривое зеркало нашего мира, где корпорации правят балом (а что, у нас сейчас разве по-другому?). 
Нам просто показывают будущее, то к чему нас сейчас ведут сильные мира сего - человеческая жизнь не стоит ни гроша, а чтоб занять место под солнцем - придётся изрядно потрудиться, да и Господин Случай тоже решает. Как бы там ни было, сюжет весьма интересный и захватывающий. Жаль только то, что разработчики не добавили больше вариабельности результатов от принятых тобой по мере прохождения игры решений. Сама же атмосфера весьма мрачная. Город хоть и светится яркими огнями, но ты понимаешь, что за яркими неоновыми вывесками города скрывается бездонные пустота и безысходность. Хотя, забавных моментов, порой тоже в игре хватает, куда уж без этого.

Графика в игре: Что тут сказать, если игра до сих пор считается одним из лучших "бенчмарков" для замера производительности системы? 
Сам лично я играл на ультранастройках с лучами и путями (карта выдаёт стабильные 50-60 fps), но... При этом, так же радует и тот факт что разработчики подумал и о людях с более слабыми системами, и настройки графики весьма гибкие.

Геймплей: весьма вариативный. 
Ты можешь просто спокойно любоваться красотами Найт-Сити и прилегающих к нему территорий. Исследовать город (весьма интересное для меня занятие, в мире полно различных пасхалок, скрытых мест и прочего. Да и сам мир в игре многомерен), исследовать пустоши. Кому-то это может показаться скучным, мне же было интересно. Слушать разговоры пешеходов, смотреть новости, наблюдать за NPC. 
А можешь ввязаться в бой с очередными бандитскими выродками (по мере выполнения заказов порой просто волосы дыбом становились от того что вытворяли те или иные уроды и нередко хотелось выпилить их самолично, что игра даёт возможность сделать в полной мере), и тогда понеслась м*да по кочкам. Бои тут так же вариативны: можешь вырезать всех тихо, а можешь переть напролом как берсерк, расчленяя всех и каждого встречного. Благо вариантов убийства и нейтрализации супротивников игра предлагает массу. Всё это исходит из ветки и системы прокачки, которую ты выберешь. Тут вариативности тоже хватает: от тихих стелса с клинками и нетранерства, до использования пушек больших и малых калибров. В общем, выбор есть и он весьма обширный.

Как было сказано ранее: 1500+ часов о многом могут сказать. Я и сам был крайне удивлён времени, проведённому в этой игре, когда впервые взглянул на счётчик времени в Стиме. Оно летит для меня незаметно. Игра очень атмосферная и буквально погружает игрока в свой мир с самых первых его шагов.

Заключение: 
Есть такие игры, которые когда пройдёшь впервые - хочется забыть, чтоб перепройти их заново, и ощутить те самые первые эмоции. Да, не смотря на такое количество часов, проведённых в ней она по прежнему дарит эмоции, не смотря на то, что я, наверное, знаю практически всё в ней. Возможно это странно, но периодически что-то заставляет меня возвращаться в неё снова и снова. Такие игры, которые не отпускают тебя, или же хоть отпускают, но ненадолго. И Cyberpunk 2077 одна из таких немногих игр. Да, не Киберпанком единым я живу, но... Кто бы что ни говорил: для меня эта игра - Шедевр!

Ahí luego, чумбы!
看了边缘行者入坑，玩了100个小时最大的感受就是：顶级的配乐----能让人起鸡皮疙瘩，主线还算完整，支线感觉没做完，尤其是市长人物更能体现出来。人生的第一款游戏，热爱到家里即使没钱买电脑也要每天早上蹬自行车去离家5公里的网吧体验上一把，总体来说是瑕不掩瑜的作品，值得体验👍

Cara… o que é Cyberpunk 2077? Sinceramente? Eu comprei esse jogo faz um bom tempo, mas nunca joguei porque não sou fã da temática cyberpunk. Broxei ainda mais depois do fiasco do lançamento, nem o anime eu quis dar uma chance. Mas quando eu dei uma chance ao anime... ai meu amigo, esse jogo virou obrigatório pra mim, só quem viu entenderá. O jogo melhorou demais também desde o lançamento. Então se eu curti demais esse game, se você for fã da temática, vai se apaixonar.

Cyberpunk foi provavelmente o maior soco no estômago que eu recebi nos últimos anos dentro de um jogo. Eu terminei meu primeiro final com muito ódio. Ódio real. Ódio dos personagens, do final, das escolhas e até do próprio jogo. O jogo despertou um ódio que eu nem sabia que tinha. E o mais engraçado é que depois eu lembrei de uma frase que o personagem V disse: "quando eu estava vendo as memórias dele ,tudo que eu senti era ódio. é como se eu odiasse tudo" E eu comecei a rir sozinho no final porque lembrei disso, pois foi exatamente assim que me senti na rota que escolhi. Fazer a pessoa do outro lado da tela sentir algo tão forte a ponto de carregar aquilo mesmo depois de desligar o jogo é uma coisa que eu nunca tinha presenciado nessa intensidade, e talvez seja isso que faz Cyberpunk ser um jogo absurdo.

O mais importante: O que me fez jogar tantas horas foi a imersão. E eu não tô falando daquela imersão genérica que todo jogo promete. No Cyberpunk você não cria um personagem pra jogar com ele. Você não cria o(a) V. Você é o(a) V. É isso que faz você abrir o jogo e depois de um tempo perceber uma coisa estranha: você não tá jogando em Night City, você está em Night City. Fazendo missão aleatória, gastando horas mexendo em gráfico, fazendo side quest pra ficar forte ou simplesmente andando pela cidade.

Eu joguei a DLC também. Apesar de não ser obrigatória, se conseguir pegar, compra, é altamente recomendável. Ela complementa muito o jogo e sinceramente? Depois de terminar Cyberpunk eu não consigo imaginar a experiência sem ela. É uma das melhores DLCs que joguei até hoje , é quase outro jogo dentro do próprio cyberpunk.

E vou falar uma última coisa: eu achei que estava zerando um jogo. No fim eu tava me despedindo de uma vida após terminar o game. E se você leu por algum milagre até aqui e ta perguntando se eu recomendo? Bem, se você realmente leu o que eu disse e ta esperando eu recomendar, então é porque o game não é pra você.

Хороший сюжет, интересный открытый мир, хорошая система RPG, а главное не муторная, легкая платина.

А теперь главный вопрос: что такое этот ваш киберпанк?

夜之城没有真正的光明。霓虹闪烁，却映照不出温暖；街道潮湿，却掩不住孤独。这不仅是一座城市，而是一部活生生的赛博朋克编年史。
	V 的存在是一种无声的悲剧。每一次拔枪、潜行、黑客入侵，都不仅是生存手段，更是一种对命运的抗争。义体的金属关节在动作中低鸣，提醒你——身体可以强化，灵魂却无法修复。战斗不仅是技巧，更是与孤独对峙的仪式，每一次交锋都在重新书写生存的代价。
	剧情线如暗河般蜿蜒：早期的选择在未来悄然回响，人物的理想与渴望常被现实碾碎。强尼·银手的孤独、街头边缘人的挣扎、帮派与企业的阴谋，都在提醒你，这里没有绝对的正义，只有选择后的伤痕与无法言说的遗憾。每一个任务、每一次偶遇，都让夜之城充满了鲜活感：雨水打在金属与皮革上，折射出的光影冰冷扭曲；霓虹在水洼里映出被拉长的梦，仿佛提醒你——未来的光亮，也许注定会折射出阴影。
	当游戏推进到最后，屏幕缓缓切入制作组名单，霓虹与城市的喧嚣退去，只剩下淡淡的电子光晕。《Never Fade Away》的旋律缓缓响起，像从遥远的记忆中飘来的告别。名单滚动间，全息电话亮起——V的好友们出现在虚拟空间里，他们声音颤抖，带着关切与惋惜。短短的片刻，却让孤独和失落具象化，提醒你：即便在钢铁与霓虹交织的世界，情感仍然真实而脆弱。
	《赛博朋克2077》的悲剧不仅在于夜之城的残酷，也在于每一次选择后的错过与无可挽回的命运。那首歌和那通全息电话，将V的终章定格成一曲无声的哀歌——短暂、深沉，却在玩家心中久久回响，让人无法释怀。
Es un milagro de Dios que mi super PC de la nasa adelantada a un millón de años luz pueda ejecutar tremenda obra de arte 11/10

Cyberpunk 2077 had a rocky release due to the premature release, but it eventually redeemed itself with the release of Phantom Liberty along with several quality of life improvements. Now, it would be safe to say that Cyberpunk 2077 is in a playable state, although not perfect by any means. It is still possible run into some bugs, and the AI still needs adjustments, especially for the pedestrians. They simply jump in front of the car, causing NCPD to tail you unnecessarily.

However, they’re not too big of a problem to take you out of the experience. Night City looks astonishing, the combat feels great, and the story pulls you in just fine. Can’t forget about the amazing soundtrack as well. All in-game soundtrack and the radio stations are simply bangers that they got me hooked the day I started playing it.

As I’ve mentioned before, the game used to be a wreck, therefore; I purposely waited for them to sort out the kinks before playing, and I don’t regret that decision. It was marketed as an open world RPG initially and I believe it still qualifies as one despite many claim it isn’t. There’s an in-depth character customization, and many skill trees to choose from as well as implants that can personalize your experience according to your tastes and needs.

Depending on which skill tree you chose to upgrade, the gameplay changes drastically. They can even be combined with others to create a hybrid build. Furthermore, Phantom Liberty adds the additional Relic Tree to flesh out the already existing builds. There are simply too many possibilities that it would be unfair to say that there isn’t enough to lead a unique experience.

The story is where the game shines best, as many have already stated before. However, the main missions are mostly focused on world-building, thus they lack enough combat to make you properly appreciate what the game truly offers. Nevertheless, Cyberpunk 2077 isn’t a game that should be breezed through. One should tackle side quests too if they want to unlock the other endings and see the bond between V and other characters.

Johnny Silverhand’s story also gets fleshed out and told mostly through side quests. Many also claim that Keanu Reeves’ involvement ruined what could’ve been a great game otherwise, but I’m inclined to disagree with that take. While it is true that his involvement happened quite late in development and led to many rewrites in the script, I’d say it turned out great.

The only aspect that left me a bit disappointed was the fact that V’s background doesn’t impact that story as much as I had hoped. The only truly different part, depending on your background, is the introduction mission and how you ended up in Night City and met Jackie Welles. All in all, Cyberpunk 2077 is a must-play. You could also watch Edgerunners to get a better grasp on the universe it is based on.

[h1]TL;DR[/h1]
Despite having a rocky headstart, Cyberpunk 2077 managed to pull itself together with Phantom Liberty as well as other patches. It offers a great storyline, strong combat, and a heavily customizable experience. The soundtrack is also noteworthy, and the universe it was built upon feels real in all aspects!

[h1] CHOOM, THIS IS THE MASTERPIECE OF NIGHT CITY [/h1]

[b][i]  Conquered Night City with a flawless 100% completion badge. I didn't just play this game; I lived, chromed-up, and burned this city to the ground alongside Johnny Silverhand.

Let’s be real, its launch was a complete cyber-disaster, but CD Projekt Red turned this ship around so hard that it evolved into an absolute legendary ride. From the tragic, existential depths of the main story to the unmatched, neon-soaked dystopian atmosphere, it is now a pure masterpiece. The massive Phantom Liberty DLC takes the experience to an entirely new level, dragging you into the gritty, high-stakes spy thriller world of Dogtown with an unforgettable narrative.

But amidst all the chaos, betrayal, and chrome, true love found its way in the badlands. Finding real connection with Panam Palmer, fighting alongside the Aldecaldos, and ending this journey with her by my side was the ultimate peak of this story. If you haven't achieved everything and driven into the sunset with Panam, you haven't truly seen Night City. A masterpiece that owns a permanent crown on my profile.

Wake the f**k up, Samurai. We have a city to burn. 9/10  [/i][/b]

[h1] AMAZING GAME [/h1]

Cyberpunk is exactly my kind of game. FPS + Fast-Paced + Dialogue Choices + Looter-Shooter + RPG + Open World + Futuristic + Romance, it's the absolute ultimate combination. The gunplay is pretty solid, the story is engaging, and the dialogue choices are interesting yet a little bit risky, so you can't just click through them without thinking.

[h2] The World & Loot [/h2]
The characters are interesting, though they could still use a bit more development and deep-diving into their backstories. Still, I love Johnny. He's probably one of the best companions in gaming since Elizabeth from Bioshock Infinite. He’s like an uncle who tells me what to do and warns me whether something is safe or not.

The looter-shooter mechanics are absolutely rewarding af. You don't have to level up or anything just to equip a weapon, which makes exploration feel so much more worthwhile. It’s unlike those other games where you’re like, [i]"Wow, I found this great looking gun after exploring hour after hour, finally!"[/i] only for the game to say, [i]"Congratulations on finding this gun! Now go waste some more time leveling up just to equip it."[/i] Like, bruh~.

Some people wanted this game to be in TPP, but not me. I’m relieved it turned out to be FPP. I play Fallout and Skyrim in first-person too, and even for survival games, I always prefer and enjoy FPP way more.

[h3] My Current Hoard: [/h3]
[*] 150 Weapons
[*] 55 Melee Weapons
[*] 484 Clothing Items
[*] 14 Vehicles
[*] 200+ Quests done in the base game alone (and I still haven't collected everything! :))

[h2] Enough for the review now it's yapping time, my journey to playing this game (Cuz it's such a memory....) [/h2]

I remember when this game blew up everywhere. I saw the memes, my favorite YouTuber uploading build videos, and all the complaints about it being a buggy mess in terrible shape. 

I got curious and searched YouTube for the gameplay, and damn, it looked cool af. I realized this was the exact game I had wanted for a long time. I wanted to play it so bad, but I didn't have a device that could run it. Even though it was buggy, I just didn't care. I ended up turning off the video and told myself:

[quote] "I will definitely play this game in the future." [/quote]

I stopped watching because I knew I'd play it eventually, and I didn't want to spoil anything. Time passed, and I kept saving money from school. It was a constant cycle: saving money > building a PC > components failing/faulty > saving money again > building a PC again > failing again > repeat.

Then, in 2024, I finally built a PC with an [b]i5-3470 + RX 460 2GB + 8GB RAM[/b]. An absolute potato, but hey, I could finally play! With the help of some optimization mods, I beat the game and the DLC with 80 hours of playtime. And you know what? My GPU literally died right after I beat it lol. At least I finally got to play it and reached my dream. I always praise this game and recommend it to friends or random people. One of my friends actually loved it and became a huge fan!

[spoiler] RIP to the RX 460 though, it died a hero's death. [/spoiler]

Як на мене, Cyberpunk 2077 це що не є ШЕДЕВР, це не просто гра, а цілий живий світ. От я мою думку Найт Сіті одночасно красиве й огидне, неонові вулиці, корпорації, бруд, музика, персонажі, усе створює реально неймовірну атмосферу. Але головне тут навіть не стрілялки і ці всі штуки типу кіберімпланти і так далі, а сам сюжет та персонажі відчуваються настільки живими, а деякі моменти буквально чіпляють за душу.
Пам'ятаю у гри був доволі проблемний старт і попри це, вона перетворилась у справжній мастерпіс.
Для мене сюжет, діалоги й свобода у проходженні роблять цю гру однією з найкращих, у які я коли-небудь грав, хоча за свій час пройшов не так вже й багато ігор

I know I’m very late to this game, but after finally completing , it was absolutely worth the wait. I don’t know how the game was at launch maybe it really was rough back then but in its current state, this game feels amazing.

The feeling of roaming around the night City is unmatched. The RPG mechanics may not be as deep as the ones as their other games, but I still loved the build variety. There are so many different builds to experiment with, which makes the gameplay even more fun. My personal favorite was the slasher build.

The NPC questlines were genuinely interesting too, and I really enjoyed the main story. The ending can honestly make you emotional. CDPR really did an incredible job turning this game into something far better than what people remember from release.

The gigs might feel repetitive for some players, but I loved exploring the map so much that I ended up completing every single one of them.

For me, this game is easily a 10/10, and I can’t wait to start the  DLC next.

哪怕时至今日，这鸟游戏也没达到宣发时鼓吹的一半，甚至还会时不时来点神秘bug让人怀疑荒坂的黑客是不是入侵我电脑了，当然可能真到了2077年它也还是这b样，但是，我依然会为了这段如梦似幻的旅程起立鼓掌，亮眼且完成度很高的世界观构筑，目眩神迷的美术风格和场景搭建，错综复杂的网状叙事，当然还有最重要的也是cdpr能成功的最大的因素：写好一个好故事、写活一个好人物。他们确实擅长从小处入手，将人物细腻复杂的心理和整体的角色弧光写的传神，我想不只是v和强尼，夜之城里哪怕是一个只沾染些许笔墨的小角色，制作组也没有放弃对他们的挖掘和塑造，尤其是上述优点是会相互呼应的，不同地区对应的建筑风格及其小细节还有日夜不停的高饱和霓虹灯诉说着这个荒诞又真实的时代背景，这样的背景又孕育和毁灭了包括主角在内的一众人物，这样的人物组成了各方势力盘根错节，而他们又是这个时代最好的代言人，我确实在这方面找不出什么缺点，事实证明，哪怕在这样一个科技发达到人能赛博永生的世界里，能击穿顶级义体和合金护甲覆盖的那颗心最有效的武器依然是人类的灵魂和感情，感谢制作组，晚安，夜之城

Par Allah, c'est l'un des meilleurs jeux de ce siècle. Histoire incroyable, personnages incroyables, intrigue incroyable, plusieurs fins incroyables, ville incroyable, graphismes incroyables, armes incroyables, missions secondaires incroyables. C'est un jeu tout simplement magnifique de A à Z.

Et l'anime avec David Martinez est incroyable aussi. À jouer absolument si vous n'êtes pas un tahan farhan qui ne fait que jouer à Fortnite et Valorant tous les jours. Vraiment, ce jeu, c'est un film.  je l'aime V et Johnny Silverhand

Adeus, V. Nunca pare de lutar.

Que jogaço.

Eu tinha encostado em Cyberpunk 2077 uns 3 anos depois do lançamento, mas nunca curti muito o universo cyberpunk e nem o estilo de gameplay. Quando via vídeos, não me pegava tanto. Porém agora, quase 6 anos depois, resolvi dar uma chance… e caraca, que experiência absurda.

Absolute cinema.

Tive alguns bugs durante a gameplay, mas nada grave, carros bugados, cenários estranhos e coisas pontuais. O único problema real foram os crashes; nessa versão mais recente o jogo está bem instável em alguns momentos, e isso foi o que mais incomodou.

Agora sobre o jogo em si: valeu demais ter jogado.

A história é profunda, pesada e extremamente marcante. Ela trabalha temas como confiança, liberdade, identidade, medo da morte e, principalmente, laços humanos, e pra mim esse é o ponto mais forte da obra.

Você é o V, mas ao mesmo tempo constrói quem ele realmente é. Suas escolhas importam não só pelo final, mas pelas relações que você cria com cada personagem. Desde Takemura até um simples nômade em uma side quest qualquer, todos parecem ter vida própria.

Poucos RPGs conseguiram me passar essa sensação de que os personagens continuam existindo mesmo quando você não está olhando. Eles têm seus próprios problemas, objetivos, ambições e conflitos. Quando suas vidas se cruzam com a sua, aquilo realmente importa. Isso dá um peso emocional absurdo para a jornada.

E os finais são excelentes. Cada um carrega uma perspectiva diferente sobre tudo que você viveu até ali. Não vou listar nenhum para não estragar a experiência de quem ainda vai jogar.

Uma coisa que deixou tudo ainda melhor foi jogar pesquisando cada detalhe. Fui atrás da história das gangues, das corps, dos personagens, das regiões e até de pequenas missões secundárias. Isso mudou completamente minha experiência.

Entender o contexto de cada lugar, o motivo de certas decisões e o peso por trás de cada personagem fez tudo ficar muito mais profundo. Eu joguei praticamente com o Google Gemini do lado e também assisti vários vídeos sobre lore, e sinceramente recomendo demais fazer isso. Night City fica ainda maior quando você entende o que realmente está acontecendo ali.

Sobre a cidade e ambientação: Night City é quase perfeita.

A cidade realmente parece viva, crimes acontecendo, polícia contra gangues, NPCs discutindo, pequenas histórias secundárias e personagens vivendo seus próprios problemas. Cada região tem sua própria identidade, e as gangues têm presença e personalidade muito bem construídas.

Mas ainda existe uma sensação de “quase”. Em alguns momentos a cidade parece meio morta: faltam interiores, muitos NPCs são rasos ou repetidos, e isso quebra um pouco a imersão.

Também senti falta de um sistema mais profundo com corporações e principalmente com as gangues. Seria incrível poder construir reputação real com facções, criar rivalidades, desbloquear missões exclusivas e fazer o mundo reagir mais às suas escolhas.

Nas mecânicas, porém, o jogo entrega demais.

Você pode criar builds extremamente fortes e totalmente diferentes entre si. A árvore de habilidades permite vários estilos, stealth, hacking, força bruta, lâminas (build que usei, de samurai, OP demais), armas pesadas e por aí vai.

E o sistema de cyberwares (os famosos cromos) é uma das melhores partes. Não é só equipar itens melhores, você literalmente modifica seu corpo. Quer desacelerar o tempo? Dá. Quer virar uma máquina de combate? Dá. Quer hackear tudo sem sacar uma arma? Também dá.

Isso faz a progressão ser muito mais interessante, porque seu personagem evolui de verdade dentro daquele universo.

No geral, minha nota é 4.5/5.

O motivo de não ser um 5/5 está justamente nesse sentimento de “quase”. Cyberpunk 2077 é um quase perfeito mundo vivo.

una obra maestra, tanto el juego base como el DLC, con bugs visuales nada grave, una historia y jugabilidad que te atrapan, despues de haber terminado el juego me quedo con la frase "no hay finales felices en Night City", finales agridulces que te dejan un vacio profundo

Прекрасная игра которую очень тяжело отпускать после прохождения. Великолепный сюжет, прекрасные персонажи и наратив. Стилистика заслуживает отдельной похвалы. Всё проведённое время в игре оставило только положительные эмоции. Отдельно про DLC, оно просто шедевр. Понравилось даже больше основного сюжета, ни в коем случае его не пропускайте!!! Игре ставлю твёрдую 9/10. Не 10 из 10 чисто потому-что баги всё же до сих пор встречаются пускай и не жёсткие.

Классная игрушка. Баги со временем воспринимаются как часть мира, уже как забавная фича. Единственное, что мне хотелось это больше взаимодействий с партнёром. После прохождений всех квестов и телефонных разговор с ним (ней) + переписок, партнёр становится как кукла. Одни и те же диалоги, ничего нового, никаких новых взаимодействий. И ещё в барах/клубах было бы прикольно приходить и просить бармена налить, и он тебе наливает сразу (такой бар есть у Хэндса), а не заходить в меню торговли и выбирать, что купить. Я хочу сидеть в баре и пить, как последний чумба! А не покупать и потом забыть о его существовании в своем кибер инвентаре.

Oyun tam anlamıyla bir başyapıt. Night City’nin atmosferi, karakterlerin derinliği, hikayenin duygusal ağırlığı ve oynanış özgürlüğü beni fazlasıyla etkiledi.Söylendiği gibi herhangi bir çökme ya da bir bug ile de karşılaşmadım.

Özellikle V, Johnny Silverhand ve yan karakterlerin hikayeleri çok başarılı yazılmış. Verdiğiniz kararların sonuçlarını görmek oyuna ayrı bir anlam katıyor. Ana hikaye kadar yan görevler de son derece kaliteli hatta bazı yan görevler birçok oyunun ana görevlerinden bile daha etkileyici. 

Görsel tasarım müzikler ve şehir atmosferi kusursuz. Şehirde gezerken bile kendinizi yaşayan bir dünyanın içindeymiş gibi hissediyorsunuz. (Özellikle geceleri motor sürmek ayrı bir keyiff) Phantom Liberty ile birlikte bambaşka bir seviyeye çıkmış. Ana hikayeyi bitirdikten sonra Phantom Liberty DLCsini kesinlikle almanızı tavsiye ederim.

Şu ana kadar oynadığım en iyi oyunlardan. Rol yapma oyunlarını seven herkesin mutlaka deneyimlemesi gerek.

Ufak bir öneri: Oyunu sindire sindire yavaş yavaş oynayın çünkü bitirdiğinizde gerçekten üzülüyorsunuz :))))

Relancer le jeu une fois par an, faire une run de nomade, baver sur la beauté de l'open world et les musiques, se balader en moto un peu partout dans Night City, ne pas rejoindre Hanako à l'embers, fermer le jeu. C'est tellement peak

So I never played this game at launch and only played it years later. Ngl, I didn't want to play through it at first but after buying it i told myself to play if for at least 3 days. It turned into a 3 month obsession. I didn't know it was a game where you could get different endings. This game was genuinely somewhat responsible for ending a 2 year relationship i had with my fiance because of how real if felt and how nihilistic i became. one of the most real games i have ever played. no game comes remotely close to being as immersive or having characters that feel so genuinely alive. and the people who tossed this game to the side after a few crashes and never gave it a second chance, don't deserve the masterpiece that it is now. legit a 10/10 game.

누구나 이 게임의 결점을 이야기할 수는 있습니다.
하지만 누구도 이토록 애정을 담아 게임을 만들 수는 없을 것입니다.

사이버펑크 2077은 분명 많은 결점을 안고 출시된 게임이었고, 지금도 완전히 지워지지 않는 아쉬움이 남아 있습니다.
그럼에도 개발진이 이 작품에 쏟아부은 사랑과 노력만큼은 단 한순간도 의심해 본 적이 없습니다.

아마 개발이 진행될수록, 개발진 스스로도 수없이 생각했을 것입니다.
‘아, 이건 이렇게 만들면 안 되는데.’
하지만 게임은 한 명의 예술가가 오롯이 자신의 고집만으로 완성할 수 있는 장르가 아닙니다.
글이나 그림, 혹은 만화는 어쩌면 가능할지도 모르겠습니다.

그러나 게임, 그리고 영화와 같은 종합예술은 다릅니다.
음악과 미술, 서사와 연출, 기술과 시스템.
수많은 요소가 얽혀 하나의 ‘아름다움’을 만들어냅니다.

사람들은 왜 예술을 만들까요.
결국 아름다움을 추구하기 위해서일 것입니다.
그리고 사람들은 그 아름다움에 감동하고, 가치를 부여하며, 즐거움을 느낍니다.

하지만 예술은, 아직 아름다울지조차 확신할 수 없는 상태에서 막대한 비용과 시간을 요구합니다.
특히 게임 같은 종합예술은 더욱 그렇습니다.

그리고 예술에 돈이 얽히는 순간, 시간이라는 가장 냉혹한 비용 역시 함께 따라옵니다.
가능성과 가치가 보이더라도, 결국 어느 순간에는 반드시 ‘완성’되어야 하니까요.

문득 그런 생각이 들었습니다.
사이버펑크 2077은 어쩌면, 우리가 쉽게 헤아릴 수 없는 수많은 사정 속에서 조금 이르게 세상에 나온 작품이 아니었을까 하고요.

물론 여기에 대한 반박도 많을 것입니다.
그리고 대부분의 반박은 결국 하나의 질문으로 귀결됩니다.

“정말 그게 최선이었나?”

사이버펑크의 주인공 V와의 여정을 끝내고 싶지 않아, 엔딩을 미룬 채 보낸 시간이 어느덧 2년.
이제는 그 질문에 답할 수 있을 것 같습니다.

적어도 결과만 놓고 본다면, 그것은 분명 그들의 최선이었다고.

그들이 상상한 사이버펑크 세계는, 그들이 할 수 있는 최선의 방식으로 이 게임 안에 구현되어 있습니다.
시간과 비용, 시대와 갈등, 그리고 수많은 고민 끝에 도달한 형태로 말입니다.

문득 영화 닥터 스트레인지의 한 장면이 떠오르기도 했습니다.
마지막 전투는 모두를 완벽하게 구해낸 싸움은 아니었지만, 적어도 그들이 도달할 수 있었던 최선의 선택이었으니까요.

최근 공개된 붉은 사막을 보며, 사이버펑크 2077의 초기 모습이 떠올랐습니다.
그래서 이렇게 뒤늦은 리뷰를 남기게 되었습니다.

오늘 저는 빈센트와 발레리와의 여정을 마무리했습니다.
그리고 새로운 세계로 떠날 준비를 하고 있습니다.

스칼리츠의 헨리, 재의 귀인, 그리고 조엘과 엘리까지.
정든 세계와 친구들에게 작별을 고하는 일은 언제나 쉽지 않네요.

그래도 앞으로 이 게임을 플레이할 사람들이, 과거의 상처와 악평만을 보고 나이트 시티로 향하는 발걸음을 멈추게 되는 것은 아쉽다고 생각했습니다.
그래서 이렇게 두서없는 글을 남기게 되었네요.

좋은 영화 한 편을 보고 극장을 나설 때처럼, 오래 마음에 남는 무언가를 느꼈습니다.

당신도 사이버시티 속에서 그 기분을 느낄 수 있으면 좋겠습니다.

긴 글 읽어주셔서 감사합니다.

Mutlaka alın oynayın, cyberpunk temasını hali hazırda sevdiğim için o kadar içine çekti ki beni boş vaktim olsa da tekrar farklı bir build ile bitirsem diyorum. Hikayesi zaten şahane, müzikleri yerine göre ekrana boş boş baktırıyor, combat sistemi de benim hoşuma gitti, sadece sürüş dinamiği biraz geri ama kötü de denilmez.

Öncelikle ben bu oyunu 2020’de ön sipariş olarak aldım ancak o zamanlar sistemimin yetersizliğinden dolayı oynayamadım. Bu sene sistemi yeniledikten sonra oynayabildim. Evet, tam 6 yıl sürdü.

Çevre ile başlamak istiyorum anlatmaya. Şehrin yapısı, görüntüsü ve şehirde bulunan atmosferden bahsedeceğim. Cyberpunk 2077 için cidden mükemmel bir dünya yaratmışlar. Her yerde bir şey oluyor ve şehrin canlı olduğunu size çok iyi bir şekilde yansıtıyorlar. Örnek vermek gerekirse; polislerle kapışan çeteler, yaralanmış bir NC vatandaşını kurtarmaya gelen sağlık ekipleri, olay yeri inceleme ekipleri, çetelerin kendi içerisinde takılma alanları, restoranlarda, kafelerde ve büfelerde yemek yiyip içen insanların varlığı ve bunun gibi saymakla bitiremeyeceğim bir sürü unsur var. Kısacası yoğun, kalabalık ve yaşayan bir şehir atmosferini mükemmel derecede vermişler. Arabaya atlayıp saatlerce şehrin sokaklarında gezilebilecek bir oyun yapmışlar, tebrik ederim bu konuda.

Oynanış tarafında çoğunlukla aklınızdakini yapmanıza izin veriyor oyun. Oynanış olarak bir derinliği var, bölüm tasarımları da aynı şekilde buna elverişli bir şekilde tasarlanmış. Sadece ana hikâye değil, en basit yan görev veya içerik bile buna göre hazırlanmış. O konuda bir sıkıntı yok ancak oyunda bir süreden sonra inanılmaz güçlü oluyorsunuz. Oyun bir anda kedi-fare oyununa dönüyor; bir zorluk veya mücadele hissi kalmıyor. Bunun biraz daha dengeli dağıtılmasını isterdim. Kaldı ki 2.0 ile gelen düzenlemelerde karakterimiz nerflenmiş olmasına rağmen hâlâ çok güçlü.

Yan hikâyeler dışında kalan yan içerikler ise maalesef biraz sığ. Oyundaki onlarca NCPD tarayıcı görevi, sanki bir Ubisoft oyunu oynuyormuşum gibi outpost temizliyormuş hissi veriyor. Ama işbitiricilerin verdiği kısa süreli işler daha derinlemesine ve içerik olarak daha dolu görevler sunuyor. Oyun biraz kendi açığını orada kapatıyor. Özellikle kısa süreli işlerde, görevlerin sonuna geldiğinizde sizi biraz daha zorlayıcı objective’lerle karşılaştırıp farklı yaklaşımlarda bulunmanıza olanak sağlayan görev yapıları verilmiş. O konuda bayağı emek harcanmış.

Başta belirttiğim gibi karakteriniz için bir sürü farklı oynanış şekli var. Oyunu tamamen gizlilik odaklı, çatışma odaklı, yakın dövüşçü veya hacker olarak oynayabilirsiniz. Yaptığınız build’e göre alacağınız özel pasifli silahlarla beraber oynanışı çok farklı yerlere çekebilmek mümkün.

Boss savaşları için maalesef pek iyi şeyler diyemeyeceğim, özellikle de ana oyunda. Çünkü bir zorlukları veya etkileyicilikleri yok. Son boss hariç, onda da oyun biraz hileye başvurmuş ama spoiler olabileceği için anlatmayacağım. Aklımda kalan bir tane bile unutulmaz boss savaşı olmadı oyunda. O konuda aşırı zayıf kalmışlar maalesef. İnsan böyle bir evrende ciddi anlamda havalı boss savaşları bekliyor ama maalesef zayıf. Şimdi bunu yazarken aklıma bir tane geldi; DLC başlangıcındaki sinematik boss savaşı. Ama dediğim gibi, çok akılda kalıcı bir boss’u yok bu oyunun.

Araba mekaniklerini ayrı ele alacağım çünkü kafam girsim bu mekaniklere. Oyunda adam akıllı kullanabileceğiniz bir tane araba var, o da oyunun en pahalı aracı. Diğerleri sanki sürekli araçların arkası kayacak şekilde yapılmış. Motorlar biraz daha kabul edilebilir ve kullanışlı ama arabalar için aynısı geçerli değil. Bir de o kadar uçan araç var, bir kereye mahsus bile kullandırmadılar. İnsan bari tek bir bölümlüğüne koyar.

Hikâye kısmında zamanında “Yaptığımız seçimlerin çok büyük etkileri yok.” diye yorumlar okumuştum. Oyunu bitirdikten sonra şunu diyorum: Bu yorumu yapan insanlar gerçekten gerizekâlı. Hem ana hikâyede hem yan hikâyelerde yaptığınız seçimler ciddi anlamda fark yaratıyor. Yaptığınız seçimler; sonradan göreceğiniz, konuşacağınız kişilerin diyaloglarını ve hatta oyundaki varlıklarını bile değiştiriyor. Oyunu farklı şekillerde bitirmenin bir sürü yolu var. Ben bilmem kaç kez “Acaba bunu deseydim ne olurdu?” diyerek kayıt geri aldım ve her seferinde gerçekten farklı yollara çıktığını gördüm. Hem bulunduğum görevde hem de sonradan karşıma çıkan yan görevlerde farklı gidiş yolları veya engeller olarak karşıma çıktı. Bu konuda kesinlikle övgüyü hak ediyor oyun ve o saçma yorumlara sakın aldanmayın. Biraz merakınız varsa benim gibi deneyerek oynarsanız bayağı ilgi çekici oluyor.

Karakterimiz V’den de biraz bahsedecek olursak; şahsen V’yi 3 farklı kişilikte oynayabiliyorsunuz diye düşünüyorum. Benim yakaladığım kadarıyla; biri daha agresif ve ergence tavırlara sahip bir V, diğeri daha mantıklı yaklaşabilen bir V, sonuncusu ise daha soğuk, kimseyi umursamayan ve sadece parasına bakıp yoluna devam eden bir V. Tabii bu da  sizin oyunu nasıl oynamak isterseniz ona göre ilerleyebilirsiniz

Ana hikâyeye rush atarsanız çok uzun bir oyun değil, hatta bayağı kısa aslında. Ama inanın, böyle bir oyunda ana hikâyeye rush atmak istemezsiniz. Gerçekten çok farklı yan hikâyeler ve içerikler var, yapmanızı tavsiye ederim. Hem oyunun dünyasıyla ilgili bir sürü şey öğreniyorsunuz hem de bayağı ilgi çekici oluyor.

Grafikler taş gibi, ne diyeyim yani. 6 yıllık oyun ama şu anda bile çoğu oyundan daha iyi gözüküyor. Bu arada hayatımda gördüğüm en iyi yüz modelleri bu oyunda olabilir. Karakterlerin alnındaki kırışıklıklara kadar işlemiş adamlar. Görsel anlamda müthiş iş çıkarmışlar.

Kısaca toparlamak gerekirse; oyun güzel. Hikâyesine genel bir tablodan bakarsan aşırı mükemmel değil ama sürüklüyor ve merak ettiriyor insanı. Çevre muazzam, canlı ve yaşayan bir şehir algısı yaratılmış, bayağı başarılı. Grafikler taş gibi. Oynanış farklı tarzlara göre güzel dizaynlanmış. Araç mekanikleri ise zort. Yapay zekâ tarafında çatışmadaki NPC’ler fena değil; sizi sıkıştırmaya veya olduğunuz yerden çıkarmaya yönelik bir sürü farklı yol deneyebiliyorlar. Ama aynısını yolda yürüyen NPC’ler için diyemem. GTA 3’ten hallice yapay zekâları var. Araba yanlarından geçince kendilerini arabanın önüne atıyorlar. Kaç kere bu yüzden polis peşime takıldı.

Oyun hâlâ bug’lı arkadaşlar, ona göre alın. İlk zamanlarındaki gibi oyunu kıran bug’larla karşılaşmadım ama bir sürü farklı tarzda bug gördüm. Havada duran düşmanlar, yerin içine giren NPC’ler veya durduğunuz yerde patlayan araçlar gibi örnekler var. Ama oyunu ciddi anlamda kıran hiçbir durumla karşılaşmadım.

Evet, aklıma bu kadar geldi. Benden 8.25/10 aldı. Kötü asla değil ama can sıkıcı bazı unsurları da yok değil. Biraz daha traşlanmış bir oyun olsaydı kesinlikle daha yüksek verirdim, orası kesin.

 沿著平坦空曠的沙灘向北（大帝國商場方向）一路橫掃。
此處地形毫無遮蔽，最適合使用 阿修羅狙擊槍 
進行超遠距離全自動盲狙爆頭，
極速狂飆 「斬首」與「竄網使」 技能等級 [🌐]


⚠️血腥暴力｜請斟酌觀賞🔞▼

🍷 聖地二：【黑伍德格蘭：瓦倫提諾小巷】
導航路線： 
傳送至 〔H3 巨型高樓〕 或 〔格蘭公寓住宅〕 快速傳點。

修煉核心： 
由數個複雜街區組成的精華小巷。
這裡聚集了夜城防禦力最高、
經驗值最肥厚的野生瓦倫提諾幫。

利用空中衝刺高速切入，
拔出 「涅槃＋覺」 施展流血吸血重擊，
強行觸發 「劍聖處決」 割草，瘋狂衝高 「忍者」 等級 [🌐]

🛠️ 第二階段：以戰養戰與素材套利
在兩大聖地獵殺野生怪時，
將進入不缺錢、不缺聲譽的完全體狀態。

將武器與義體強化至天花板：
高階素材以戰養戰：
野生怪會大量掉落 4 階（紫色）與 5 階（橘色）武器。
撿起後不要販售，全部就地「分解」。
配合技術的工程師被動加成，
能以最快速度囤積海量的 5 階物品組件 [🌐]

黃金種子備份：
當人物達到 60 級滿級、五大技能頂峰、
全套橘色神經義體塞滿、裝備全封頂時，
請手動存一個新檔，並複製該檔案至桌面或雲端備份，
將其命名為「黃金種子存檔」。

🎭 第三階段：無限迴圈（執法夜之城）
當讀取修煉完成的「黃金種子存檔」
走上街頭，就是這座城市的神。

因為保留了...
地圖上所有大大小小的支線、委託、NCPD 治安案件，
此時的夜之城並非通關後的冷清空城，
而是各大地區、大街小巷都充滿著極度危險的氣息，
以及充滿生命力的熱烈 NPC 互動。

🦅 【扮演你的賽博靈魂】
街頭漫步者： 
開著最頂級的超跑，

聽著洗腦神曲，享受純粹的沉浸感。

隱身暗殺者： 
啟動光學迷彩，
化身為不驚動任何人的鬼魅，
用近戰武士刀處決一切。

絕對執法者： 
面對任何罪犯據點，高調開怪，
用阿修羅天罰暴擊或應龍電磁大爆炸，
給夜城帶來真正的物理震撼。

放鬆心情、宣洩完生活中的所有壓力後，
「不存檔」直接離開遊戲 [🌐]

當下一次、下個月、甚至明年再次踏入遊戲，
讀取那個黃金存檔，會發現隊友都在，
一個永遠充滿活力、
永遠驚險刺激的「永生夜城」
正在原地等待你的歸來！

écouter des personnages parler de solitude,
regarder les néons sous la pluie,
rester immobile dans un ascenseur pendant qu’une musique incroyable joue,
éviter des cyberpsychos complètement détruits mentalement,
et dépenser des fortunes chez des prostituées virtuelles avant de repartir faire une crise existentielle en moto.

Cyberpunk 2077 ist eines der wenigen Open-World-Spiele, bei denen es selbst nach hunderten Stunden noch Spass macht, sich einfach durch die Welt zu bewegen. Durch Night City zu fahren und die Atmosphäre, Musik und Beleuchtung aufzusaugen, fühlt sich einfach unglaublich gut an.

Das Gameplay macht extrem viel Spass. Das Kampfsystem fühlt sich befriedigend an, die verschiedenen Builds bieten viel Freiheit und man kann seinen Spielstil stark nach seinen eigenen Wünschen anpassen.

Ich habe die PS4-Version gespielt, später die PS5-Version vor dem 2.0-Update und inzwischen auch die aktuelle Version. Insgesamt habe ich mehrere hundert Stunden in das Spiel gesteckt und spiele es trotzdem immer noch gerne.

Mein einziger wirklicher Kritikpunkt zeigt sich erst nach mehreren Durchläufen. Manche Storyabschnitte können sich beim erneuten Spielen etwas ziehen. 

Phantom Liberty ist das Sahnehäubchen obendrauf.
所有的不舍，所有的爱，所有的不甘，所有的泪，皆在夜之城。

Awesome game, a bit misunderstood and overhated at launch.

Graphics are incredible overall, especially after all the updates. Night City is one of the most detailed and immersive open worlds ever made, full of life, neon lights, crowded streets and beautiful environments. Character models and facial animations are excellent, and the combat animations feel fluid and satisfying. Some NPC behaviors can still feel a bit awkward at times, but the overall presentation is top tier.

Sound design is amazing. Weapons sound powerful, cars feel aggressive, the city ambience is immersive, and the voice acting is top tier across the board. The music is absolutely phenomenal, perfectly matching every moment, whether you're cruising through the city at night, fighting through chaos, or experiencing emotional story moments.

Gameplay is great and highly engaging. Let's start with the combat, because many people still think Cyberpunk is just "GTA with RPG elements", but it offers so much more than that. There are many different playstyles to choose from: stealth hacking, aggressive gunplay, melee combat, katanas, throwing knives, cyberware-focused builds, snipers, shotguns, netrunning, and more. Every build feels different and has strengths and weaknesses depending on your approach and preparation. Combat is fast, fluid and rewarding, especially once your build starts coming together. The movement also feels great, especially with double jumps, dashes and cyberware upgrades that make you feel increasingly powerful.
The progression system is great, allowing you to specialize heavily into your preferred playstyle. Skill trees offer meaningful upgrades that genuinely change how you approach combat and exploration. Cyberware customization is one of the best parts of the game, letting you turn your character into an unstoppable machine, stealth hacker, samurai or whatever style you prefer.
Looting and equipment are also very satisfying. Finding iconic weapons, upgrading cyberware and experimenting with builds keeps the gameplay fresh for dozens of hours.
Exploration is another huge strength. Night City feels alive and dense, with hidden events, side gigs, random encounters and environmental storytelling everywhere. Even just driving around the city at night can be immersive on its own.
The side content is surprisingly strong too. Many side quests are written with the same quality as the main story, often presenting difficult choices, morally gray situations and memorable characters. Some side quests end up being more emotionally impactful than entire main campaigns in other games.
Another great mechanic is how your choices and relationships affect quest outcomes, dialogue and endings. Some opportunities, conversations and endings are only available depending on how you played and who you connected with throughout the game.
These are only some of the great mechanics the game offers, the rest are for you to discover.

The enemies are varied and fun to fight. Different gangs use different weapons, tactics and cyberware, forcing you to adapt your approach. Boss fights and cyberpsychos can also be very memorable, especially when your build is tested properly.

The story is excellent, emotional and extremely well written. The characters are memorable, believable and full of personality. V and Johnny Silverhand have fantastic chemistry throughout the game, and many side characters leave a strong impression too. The role playing aspects are very well done, with meaningful dialogue choices, different character backgrounds and multiple endings depending on your decisions. The game constantly presents morally complex situations where there is rarely a completely right or wrong answer. Also some of the endings are brutal, so be prepared to a rollercoaster of emotions.

In short, awesome game, a bit misunderstood because of its disastrous launch. After years of updates and improvements, the game became what people originally hoped it would be. Many people still judge it based on old impressions without giving it another chance. If you enjoy immersive RPGs, great storytelling, strong world building and flexible character progression, this game is absolutely worth experiencing.

[h1] ENG [/h1]

Well, what can I say — this is a masterpiece.

I really enjoyed Cyberpunk 2077. The last time I felt this way was when I finished The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt (which makes sense since it’s the same company). Those 100+ hours flew by easily and were pure enjoyment.

I know the game had problems at launch, but now I didn’t encounter a single serious bug or glitch — everything works great.

I played on the highest difficulty, though I can’t say it felt that hard, especially once I was fully leveled up.

But this is more of a story-driven game. And the story is flawless. Both the main story and all the side quests — every quest feels like it was made with passion, and the variety is HUGE. Basically every mission has several ways to complete it. AND THAT’S AWESOME!

The main story definitely left a mark on my soul after finishing it. And sadness. “Life is priceless.” My ending: [spoiler] I attacked Arasaka with Rogue while Johnny controlled my body, and in the end I chose to live my own six months. I didn’t give my body to Johnny because it’s my life, and I have the right to live it the way I want (I know about the other endings too, and I think mine is the most logical one). [/spoiler]

The gameplay is solid, the build variety is huge, the weapons are diverse, the cars too, and all the little details are great.

I especially want to highlight the music. Cyberpunk 2077 HAS THE BEST SOUND DESIGN.

DLC:

I was also amazed by the expansion. Cyberpunk 2077: Phantom Liberty only improved the main game. As an alternative ending, it absolutely deserves to exist.

Here I got an even bigger dose of sadness, emotions, and thoughts. Because in the finale you can see that [spoiler] you became nobody, you’re not needed by anyone, everyone changed and moved on with their own path — even your best friend Viktor, after 2 years passed since the operation. The same goes for Panam and everyone else. [/spoiler]

Overall, I got incredible enjoyment from the music, the story, and the connection with the game world. With Night City.

Oyunun güzel olmasına lafım yok. Ancak zamanında fazla abartıldığını düşünüyorum, evet çok uzun süre bekledik çıkmasını ve oyun güzel ancak insanlar popüler kültürün kölesi olmuş, çıktığı dönemler oyunu 5 dakika oynamış insanlar bile sürü psikolojisiyle, kulaktan doğma bilgiler ile oyuna olumlu verdiler, ve baya pofpofladılar ancak hikayesinin aşırı ilgi çekici olduğunu söyleyemem. Yine de atmosfer, ortam kısacası açık dünya güzel tasarlanmış. Hele o binaların birbirine yakın olması, çarpık kentleşme ve ücra kıyıların köşelerin her birinde serseri hırt diyebileceğimiz kişilerin olması hem bize yabancı gelmiyor hem de zengin-fakir ilişkisini ve güç çatışmasını güzel işlemiş. Yine de söylediklerimin arkasındayım, oyun güzel fakat bugün artık kimse Cyberpunk 2077’yi konuşmuyor. Üzgünüm ama günümüzde yeni bir bilgisayar alındığında sistemin gücünü görmek adına test için kullanılan bir oyundan ötesi değil CP77.

Cyberpunk 2077 está longe de ser um jogo perfeito, mas acertou em inúmeros quesitos. Um desses pontos é o design de som; recomendo jogar com um bom fone de ouvido ou caixinhas de som, porque a experiência sonora é incrível. Das faixas durante a investigação de um crime político até o roubo de um comboio fortemente armado, é como se as músicas também contassem uma história. O ronco dos carros e das motos, a trilha sonora quando um combate estoura ou você está prestes a morrer, tudo isso é muito bem feito e não deixa a desejar.

Outro aspecto muito bacana são as animações e a qualidade gráfica. É fácil se esquecer de que já se passaram 5 ou 6 anos desde o lançamento de Cyberpunk; o jogo é muito lindo. Se você tem condições de usar o ray tracing, use, é nota 10 nesse jogo, apenas eleva a atmosfera brutal de Night City. Com relação às animações em si, é um ponto muito relevante, esqueça aqueles diálogos travados (personagem parado como um robô), comuns em RPGs da Bethesda ou da Obsidian; sempre há algo acontecendo na tela, o movimento é sempre enfatizado.

É nisso que Cyberpunk 2077 brilha, nesses pontos mais técnicos. Claro, a gameplay é fluida e divertida (tirando dirigir em ruas estreitas e congestionadas xD), e os personagens são memoráveis, mas o forte realmente está na apresentação visual e sonora.

A respeito dos defeitos... welp, se você foi uma das vítimas da CD Projekt RED e de suas campanhas de marketing para esse jogo, sabe exatamente do que estou falando. São grandes as incoerências com o que foi originalmente anunciado. Cyberpunk mais se parece com um jogo de ação e aventura com elementos de RPG do que propriamente um RPG. E você nem precisa pensar nos títulos da Bethesda para fazer comparações; o próprio The Witcher 3 faz um trabalho melhor nesse sentido. Se desde o início essa não fosse a proposta, ok... mas não foi o caso.

Outro ponto é o ritmo da trama, o pace é simplesmente abismal. Diversas atividades e quests secundárias entram em conflito com o estabelecido na campanha principal. O jogo cria um senso de urgência com a saúde de V, ao mesmo tempo em que se contradiz com inúmeras outras atividades. Até mesmo personagens de quests secundárias impõem seu próprio senso de urgência, o que só acaba somando para essa confusão no ritmo da história. Se você é alguém que presta mais atenção nesses problemas narrativos, não tem como ignorar, e é algo que vai tirar sua paz do início ao fim do jogo.

Mais um problema é a falta de agência. Em muitos momentos, basicamente não há escolha, embora o jogo tente passar a impressão de que ela existe. V, o protagonista, também tem uma personalidade bem rígida, então, se você é fã de construir seu próprio personagem, vai ficar só na vontade. Em comparação, jogos como Baldur’s Gate 3, Fallout: New Vegas e até mesmo The Outer Worlds oferecem muito mais liberdade na interação com o mundo, permitindo mudanças mais substanciais a depender de suas escolhas em estágios iniciais ou intermediários do jogo.

Também há o defeito de possuir pouca customização de veículos, dos lifepaths (seu background) não terem muito impacto, e da falta de um sistema mais complexo para as gangues e polícia de Night City.

A cidade é bonita, mas é só isso, tem a profundidade de um pires. Entendo que não é o objetivo do jogo, mas não poderia deixar de mencionar.

Como observado, o jogo tem seus prós e contras, e nessa balança eu acabo recomendando. Não está no mesmo nível de The Witcher 3, mas a CD Projekt RED conseguiu entregar algo memorável para o gênero cyberpunk.

期待了好久买了终极版，翻看不少更新日志，想着游戏应该优化完善得差不多了吧，结果成了最后悔购买的游戏，就随便碎碎念几句吧，别当真

[b] 产品质量部分 [/b]

+ 优化差、bug多、性能堪忧，好在N网的MOD能解决一部分问题，能想象吗，平均每半个小时就能碰到一个新bug，模型消失，人物漂移，卡在车上下不来，车辆鸣笛停不下来，家里的收音机奇吵无比却无法关闭，性能测试有严重问题……数不完，不数了
+ 完美继承了《巫师3》PC版的光照问题（我在GOG上玩了巫师3超过250个小时），室内环境死黑
+ 社区里反映了很久的bug坚决不修复
+ 一言难尽的简体中文本地化，举个最简单地例子，我用的是英文配音，听到街上一个小孩骂我，头顶上的翻译是“我饿了”
+ 人物是穿模的，世界中大量模型和贴图错误（有MOD可修复，MOD制作者免费帮开发人员上班）
+ CDPR启动器更是bug得一塌糊涂，每次打开，50%的bug卡死几率，最终靠跳过启动器直接启动游戏解决的

游戏是拿出来卖的产品、商品，发布多年了，连最基本的质量保证都做不到，玩家反映很久的恶性bug没人管，甚至室内光照问题都完全继承自上代游戏作品。虽然游戏肯定免不了有bug有各种各样的问题，但2077太过了，这些问题在游玩的时候时不时地提醒你，这款游戏/产品质量很不好


[b] 游戏体验部分 [/b]

+ 空洞的开放世界。必须承认，2077整个世界的设计、建模，还是非常有潜力，相当不错的，但倒反天罡地讲一句，这款游戏，浪费了这个城市的设计和建模。整座城市不过是个方便剧情上演搭建起的戏台，出了故事主线，世界便与你毫无关联，顶多摆放了好些据点供你游玩。CDPR还记得最早火线夜之城你们是怎么宣传你们的世界的吗？
+ 夜之城的开放世界游戏体验，甚至远比不上育碧的罐头开放世界，更别说育碧的开放世界罐头优化好、质量高。以早两年、2018年的刺客信条奥德赛为例，NPC会对主角的行为做出各种反应，偷鸡摸狗甚至会被居民抄起扫帚追着打，整个世界NPC的一举一动，休闲游戏、聊天对话、唱歌跳舞、市民活动，全在告诉玩家，这里是古希腊。反观夜之城，这座漂亮庞大的城市无时不刻不在提醒我，这是个死气沉沉的游戏世界，我只是在里面游玩，整个世界与我无关。超级糟糕的驾驶手感和城市加载与掉帧问题，令城市风光旅游都变得非常难受
+ 总有一些不舒服。在巫师3里（不知道后面更新的次世代版还会不会这样），商店不会刷新，虽然我不知道基于什么原因，一款现代游戏的商店会设计出不刷新这种机制，但它就这样，受着吧。2077同样继承了这一点，比如，未装备的物品没有物品分类；武器上的配件和芯片前者能拆卸后者不能，一不小心点到就得读档；再比如，你的第一套房子非常方便，出门就是传送点和售货机，但武器房的房门不是自动门，对准开关点一下才能开，买的其他房子门是自动的了，可传送和售货机又离得很远，最起码得点一下实实在在的上下个电梯；车载广播不能从设置上彻底关闭，有几首歌特别洗脑刮躁的歌经常突然蹦出来令人暴躁

设定是未来的赛博朋克世界，却没有任何设定之外的“未来”“赛博朋克”体验，一切干瘪、空洞、毫无代入感，充满了糟心的体验，某一次退出之后，虽然也会偶尔想起剧情，想起手头上的任务，游戏依旧呆在硬盘上，却再没有任何动力进去了。

TLDR: Having never played it before this, I don't have bad memories of a broken and unplayable game. Instead I was able to go in almost completely blind and enjoyed the game a lot.

The game actually surprised me quite a bit, as I was expecting a bland or shallow game (entirely due to residual internet disdain that I was exposed to), but in truth the story was great and had a lot of fun moments. The actual gameplay is really fun and more dynamic than something like Skyrim or Fallout, which are the closest games I've played to this sort of thing. Mixing fast movements with quickhacks and fun abilities makes this game really good in and out of combat. Katanas especially... those felt really fun to parry bullets with.

The driving is SO fun in this game, which is good because they love to do sidequests that involve a LOT of back and forth. Even playing on the standard difficulty I felt super overpowered for most of the game, which resulting in clearing enemy hideouts really quickly. That almost meant I spent even more time driving, which I was able to enjoy. It's especially fun when you save up enough money to get a car with tons of health and built-in machine guns, purely to cause mischief.

The game has some flaws here and there, but they're pretty minor gripes. Most of them involve slow elevators, and the fact that your character flashes you every time you change gear. Outside of that, I enjoyed the game enough that I immediately went and watched Cyberpunk Edgerunners after finishing this game, and was sad when there weren't any more quests to do. Highly recommend this if your computer has the specs to run it.

Hi zusammen,

ich muss mir mal den Frust über das neue "Heilungs-Ending" (Der Turm) aus dem DLC von der Seele schreiben. Versteht mich nicht falsch: Ich liebe die düstere "No Happy End"-Philosophie von Night City. Aber was CD Projekt Red hier mit den Romanzen (besonders Panam und Judy) abgezogen hat, ist für mich kein gutes Writing, sondern erzwungenes Drama mit dem Vorschlaghammer.

Dass V nach 2 Jahren Koma aufwacht und von den Menschen, für die man in den Nebenquests ALLES geopfert hat, eiskalt fallen gelassen und ignoriert wird, bricht komplett die innere Logik der Charaktere. Ja, Night City ist hart, aber diese Charaktere waren loyal. Sie so umzuschreiben, dass sie V nach ein paar Monaten komplett abhaken, entwertet im Nachhinein jede investierte Spielstunde in diese Beziehungen.

Das Argument, das Ende wäre sonst "zu perfekt" gewesen, zieht nicht. V verliert sowieso alles: den Legenden-Status, die Karriere als Söldner und jede Fähigkeit, jemals wieder Cyberware zu nutzen. V fängt als schwacher, wehrloser, normaler Mensch ganz unten an. Das IST bereits die Höchststrafe im Cyberpunk-Universum!

Wie man es viel besser, realistischer und TROTZDEM düster hätte lösen können:

Anstatt dass die Partner V egoistisch abservieren, hätten sie am Krankenbett stehen oder erreichbar sein müssen. Das wahre, psychologische Drama hätte dann erst begonnen:

Der verletzte Stolz von V: V war die absolute Prime-Söldnerin/der Prime-Söldner. Wie fühlt es sich an, plötzlich komplett schwach zu sein und sich von Panam (einer kampferprobten Nomad-Anführerin) oder Judy beschützen lassen zu müssen?

Die Wahl beim Spieler lassen: Das Spiel hätte V die Option geben müssen, die Beziehung selbst zu beenden. Aus Scham, aus Stolz oder aus dem Gefühl heraus, eine Last zu sein.

Das wäre emotionaler Cyberpunk in Reinform gewesen! Ein bittersüßes Ende, bei dem man merkt: Die Liebe ist zwar da, aber Vs Ego und die neue Hilflosigkeit zerstören die Beziehung von innen heraus. Das hätte dem Spieler die Kontrolle über Vs Würde zurückgegeben, anstatt uns passiv vom Spiel bestrafen zu lassen, nur weil wir die optionalen Nebenquests gespielt haben.

Я провел в Найт-Сити сотни часов, и этот город до сих пор отказывается меня отпускать. Игра прошла огромный путь, и сейчас это абсолютно монументальный шедевр с потрясающей графикой и невероятно густой атмосферой киберпанка. Сюжет держит в напряжении до самого финала, а харизматичный Джонни Сильверхенд в исполнении Киану Ривза становится буквально родным. Но больше всего меня поразили побочные квесты — они написаны настолько живо и глубоко, что грань между главной историей и историями твоих друзей полностью стирается. Добавьте к этому драйвовый саундтрек, крутую шутерную механику и вариативность в прокачке, от которой кайфуешь при каждом прохождении. Да, на релизе было тяжело, но сейчас Найт-Сити раскрылся во всей красе и дарит просто незабываемые эмоции. Если вы ждали идеального момента, чтобы ворваться — он определенно настал, этот город ждет вас.

The only thing I dislike about this game is that there isn't an infinite amount of it for me to enjoy.  I return to this game every few months, lose myself in it for a week, gush about how great it is to my friends, and then go back to the coldbox of other games like Bartmoss for another few months.

My only real gripes are that I wish there were more voice options, and I'd like a native option to narrow the field of random stats that populate when upgrading cyberware to stats that are relevant to your build.  Mods solve this problem handily, but it's something I hope they CDPR thinks about doing if this game gets a sequel.  Also, I personally am not a fan of any of the romance targets in the game; they all have some pretty crippling character flaws that would make me shy away from a serious relationship with them, so most of the time I just skip romance entirely.

9.5/10, sell me another one please -- preferably with fewer on-launch issues than this one.

There are so many things I like about this game, but the biggest is that it makes me feel better about myself as a software developer. Stacked objects will randomly fling themselves around, especially tires. Traffic randomly stops or collide, and vehicles tear off for no reason. Bodies get stretched or thrown off into the far distance. It's fun to jump into a beautiful cityscape and wreak havoc, constantly being reminded that it's just a game.
For the actual game, I'd give it a B+ these five years after its launch. It's far, far too cutscene driven. The main storyline is just small side quests between long cutscenes. I constantly press 'skip forward' to advance dialog. I've completed all side jobs and quests, gotten all of the rewards from the fixers, and completed 3 of 4 game endings, but what I really remember are the early story arcs and the feeling that I didn't really know which subplot to follow (Johnny? Takemura? Panam? Evelyn? Judy? Rogue? River?).
In that way it was like Elden Ring to me. Confusing, fun, bewildering, and eventually something I'm glad I mastered. Night City is a fun place to visit, but I wouldn't want to live there.
游戏首发时因大量 BUG、优化糟糕、内容缩水饱受诟病，口碑一度跌入谷底。但经过多年持续更新、修复 bug 并推出 DLC 后，体验大幅改善。大家普遍认可其顶尖的剧情、人物塑造、赛博朋克氛围与世界观，角色刻画立体、故事立意深刻，夜之城的沉浸感十足。

Depois de mais de 100 horas nesse jogo, posso dizer que este jogo me surpreendeu muito mais do que eu esperava.

Quando comecei a jogar, achei que seria só mais um jogo de mundo aberto futurista, mas acabei ficando completamente preso à história. Aos poucos fui criando ligação com os personagens e comecei a sentir que as minhas escolhas realmente importavam. Houve missões que me fizeram ficar vários minutos a pensar no que tinha acabado de acontecer.

Uma das melhores coisas foi explorar a Night City. Mesmo depois de dezenas de horas, eu ainda encontrava lugares que nunca tinha visto antes. A cidade parece viva, principalmente à noite, com as luzes, os anúncios gigantes e toda a atmosfera cyberpunk.

O combate também foi uma das partes que mais me divertiu. No início eu jogava de forma mais simples, mas conforme fui desbloqueando habilidades comecei a experimentar várias builds diferentes.

A história foi provavelmente a melhor parte para mim. A relação entre o V e o Johnny Silverhand foi muito mais interessante do que eu imaginava. No começo nem sempre concordava com o Johnny, mas ao longo do jogo a dinâmica entre os dois foi mudando e isso tornou a experiência muito mais marcante.

Claro que nem tudo é perfeito. Encontrei alguns bugs aqui e ali, embora nada que me impedisse de jogar. Também teve algumas vezes em que senti que algumas escolhas podiam ter tido consequências maiores.

No geral, Cyberpunk 2077 foi daqueles jogos que me fez querer continuar a jogar mesmo quando já tinha decidido desligar o PC. Muitas vezes dizia "só mais uma missão" e acabava por ficar mais uma hora jogando.

Depois de 100 horas, sinto que aproveitei praticamente tudo o que o jogo tinha para oferecer e posso dizer que foi uma das melhores experiências que tive nos últimos anos. Não é perfeito, mas é um daqueles jogos que fica na memória depois dos créditos finais aparecerem. Eu daria um 9,5/10 sem pensar muito.

I honestly am torn between recommending and not recommending this game. The gameplay itself is fantastic. It makes a lot of fun, and that is only after one playthrough of a more stealth/Quickhack based Playstyle. The looting, scenery and also just the overall atmosphere is great and brings everything I want from a Sci-fi Looter Shooter. 

My Problem is with the Endings. I feel they are all badly written and just make no sense at all. I chose the Arasaka Ending, or rather came out there where I did the first one at the rooftop without the Pills, restarted and used the other longer one where I go to the Arasaka Tower and play through the Hanako Story. 

The Ending I got there is just terrible. My Romance just ups and leaves after I called her from the Orbital station and I die 6 months after. Or I would have chosen the other route of just surrendering myself to Mikoshi, completely ignoring the entire Alt cunningham arc where I thought something happens since she told me to get into Mikoshi and she helps me after. (Did not play that ending, but from  all I read it does not end well either and you just die?)

The Endings are understandably not all roses and sunshine as is NightCity standard, but they feel forcibly bad and not coherent with the characters and the previous storytelling. 

Takes away from a great game and also makes it infuriatingly bad for a decision making game to feel like no decisions make a difference or get you to your outcome.

Overall I recommend the game for the Play aspect and surely the replayability with difference Endings, Approaches or Romance and Storylines.
Just be prepared that the Ending might leave you disappointed or feel taken out of the immersion.

Few games in history have undergone a redemption arc quite like Cyberpunk 2077. While its initial launch was famously rocky, CD Projekt RED’s unwavering commitment to their vision—culminating in the massive 2.0 update and the brilliant Phantom Liberty expansion—has officially transformed this game into a certified masterpiece.

Night City is the undisputed star of the show. It is a towering, neon-soaked vertical labyrinth that feels genuinely alive, dangerous, and intoxicating. Walking through the crowded streets of Watson or driving through the desolate Badlands offers an unparalleled level of environmental immersion. The world-building is incredibly dense, capturing the corporate-dominated, gritty essence of classic cyberpunk lore.

It is impossible to talk about the renaissance of this game without mentioning Cyberpunk: Edgerunners, the phenomenal Studio Trigger anime series. Edgerunners didn't just capture the hearts of fans; it perfectly mirrored the game's high-stakes, tragic, and adrenaline-fueled atmosphere.

The anime injected a massive wave of fresh energy into the game, making the exploration of Night City feel even more personal. Visiting locations featured in the show, listening to "I Really Want to Stay at Your House" on the in-game radio, or equipping David Martinez’s iconic jacket adds a profound layer of emotional depth for anyone who loved the series.

With the overhauled cyberware systems, revamped skill trees, and chaotic vehicle combat introduced in recent updates, the gameplay finally matches the narrative's ambition. Combat is fast, brutal, and highly customizable. Whether you want to be a stealthy Netrunner hacking enemies from afar or a sandevistan-powered solo slicing through corporate guards with a katana, the playstyles are incredibly rewarding.

At its core, the story of V and Johnny Silverhand is a deeply moving tale about legacy, identity, and survival against all odds.

Cyberpunk 2077 is no longer just a game about promises; it is a triumphant delivery of an unforgettable sci-fi RPG. If you were waiting for the right time to lose yourself in Night City—especially if you are a fan of Cyberpunk: Edgerunners—there has never been a better time to chrome up and dive in.

Купил Cyberpunk 2077 ради сюжета и красивого города.

В итоге:

40% времени — любуюсь Найт-Сити
40% — устраиваю массовый геноцид с катаной
20% — пытаюсь понять, почему я опять ехал 300 км/ч прямо в столб.

Эта игра — лучшее доказательство того, что будущее человечества будет выглядеть так:
— импланты
— корпорации
— киберпсихоз
— реклама бургеров размером с дом
— и NPC, которые пишут тебе чаще, чем реальные люди.

Первые 2 часа:
“вау, какой сюжет”

После 20 часов:
“так… а если я поставлю руки-лезвия, двойной прыжок и дробовик, то стану ли я проблемой для общества?”

Да.
Станешь.

Отдельный респект физике вождения.

Машины либо:
— едут как утюг на льду

либо:
— разгоняются так, будто у двигателя паническая атака.

И конечно же атмосфера.

Найт-Сити выглядит настолько красиво, что ты иногда забываешь выполнять миссии и просто:
— идёшь пешком
— слушаешь радио
— смотришь на неон
— случайно сбиваешь 14 человек
— продолжаешь идти дальше как ни в чём не бывало

Самое страшное — побочки.

Ты запускаешь “один маленький квест”, а через час уже эмоционально разбит сильнее, чем после реальных отношений.

И да, спасибо игре за то, что теперь при слове “чумба” у меня флешбеки.

Depois da impressão desastrosa que Cyberpunk 2077 teve no lançamento — até mesmo para quem nunca jogou — ficou aquele gosto de que ele jamais seria um bom jogo. Apesar de tudo isso, quando assisti Edgerunners pela primeira vez, senti que estava perdendo algo naquele universo, e foi aí que decidi finalmente embarcar em Night City.

Comecei a jogar pra valer depois da atualização 2.0, que ficou marcada por trazer o jogo mais próximo daquilo que ele deveria ter sido desde o lançamento. Junto disso veio a DLC Phantom Liberty. Mesmo tendo começado o jogo muito tempo depois, tive a vantagem de pegar poucos spoilers e conseguir aproveitar a narrativa praticamente intacta.

E vamos lá: essa é facilmente uma análise de um dos jogos mais narrativos que já joguei.

Primeiramente, eu queria falar do visual. A imersão no mundo cyberpunk — um gênero com o qual eu não tinha tanta familiaridade — é absurda. Esse jogo tem umas cenas de iluminação simplesmente incríveis. Dá pra passar minutos olhando pela janela vendo o neon da cidade, os carros, as pessoas... literalmente qualquer coisa nesse jogo é bonita.

Mas o maior ponto forte sempre será a narrativa. A CD Projekt já tinha mostrado isso com The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt, principalmente na forma como as missões secundárias conseguem ser tão boas quanto a campanha principal.

Tudo importa. Cada conversa é interessante, cada escolha parece ter peso, e os desfechos conseguem seguir caminhos diferentes. Infelizmente, você aprende que não existe final feliz em Night City. O jogo sempre vai deixar um gosto amargo, um nó na garganta — mas isso é Cyberpunk.

A trilha sonora também é absurda. Além das músicas que tocam no rádio durante os rolês pela cidade, o jogo mistura vários estilos diferentes que combinam perfeitamente com o cenário cyberpunk. Mas preciso destacar especialmente as OSTs de Phantom Liberty: eles sabem exatamente como usar música para transformar momentos importantes em cenas inesquecíveis.

Os personagens são maravilhosos. Você se apega, odeia, ama e entende muitos deles. A forma como a relação e a progressão do Johnny Silverhand evoluem ao longo da história é simplesmente incrível.

E meu maior destaque vai para a DLC Phantom Liberty. Você sente claramente a evolução na narrativa, no visual e na direção das missões. O nível sai de “bom” pra “bom pra caralho”. É simplesmente insano.

Comprem e joguem Phantom Liberty.

10/10 para cada final desse jogo. Não importa o quão amargos eles sejam.

Cyberpunk 2077 + Phantom Liberty — это просто разрыв.

История Сойки и Рида — ходячая драма, где нет правильного выбора, а шпионский вайб Псарни (Dogtown) пробирает до мурашек. Геймплей после патча 2.0 стал чистым адреналином. Влетать в толпу с Сандевистаном, крошить всех катаной или выжигать мозги через Черный Заслон — кайф нереальный. Это 10 из 10. Найт-Сити навсегда в сердце.

Joguei Cyberpunk 2077 pela primeira vez em 2026 e, sinceramente, fazia um bom tempo que um jogo não me prendia tanto (não da forma como Cyberpunk fez). Foram mais de 150 horas de jogo, e cada hora dentro de Night City foi especial. É raro encontrar uma experiência que consiga ser tão divertida, imersiva e emocional ao mesmo tempo.

Uma das coisas mais gratificantes de Cyberpunk foi ver como ele saiu de um lançamento tão conturbado para se transformar em uma verdadeira carta de amor aos fãs. As atualizações, melhorias — mesmo que o jogo ainda tenha alguns problemas e falhas que permanecem desde o lançamento — e a chegada da DLC Cyberpunk 2077: Phantom Liberty mostraram o carinho e o comprometimento da CD PROJEKT RED com o jogo e com a comunidade.

O que mais me marcou foi como os personagens são tratados de maneira humana. Ninguém parece genérico ou vazio. Cada personagem tem dores, sonhos, defeitos e personalidade própria, e isso faz o mundo parecer vivo de verdade.

Sem deixar de mencionar especialmente V e Johnny. Ver a relação dos dois sair de uma inimizade quase insuportável para uma amizade genuína foi uma das melhores construções que já vi em um jogo. Em muitos momentos, eles parecem irmãos — dois caras quebrados tentando encontrar algum sentido no caos e que, no fim, estão dispostos a sacrificar tudo um pelo outro.

Amei os finais. Alguns são tristes e devastadores, outros satisfatórios, e alguns conseguem deixar uma esperança genuína. Independente do caminho escolhido, todos deixam sentimentos fortes depois dos créditos.

Obrigado, CD PROJEKT RED, por essa experiência. Obrigado por não desistirem do jogo e por entregarem algo tão especial. Platinar Cyberpunk foi uma ótima experiência e uma lembrança que, a partir de agora, levarei comigo.

[h1]This is what comes to mind when someone says, “That’s a good game.”[/h1]

The gameplay offers a lot of interesting mechanics and a truly original story. The audio is [b]on another level[/b]: every background OST and in-game track fits perfectly with the moment you are experiencing.

The game is also full of small details that make the world feel alive, from random moments around Night City to little interactions and environmental details that make you smile.

[hr][/hr]

[h2]Phantom Liberty[/h2]

The DLC is also one of the best expansions I have ever played. It tells a brand-new story that still makes perfect sense within the main plot, while also adding new features, content, and details that [b]complete the overall gameplay experience[/b].

Just like the main game, Phantom Liberty is full of small moments, legendary songs, and new ways to get your heart broken.

[hr][/hr]

[h2]Edgerunners and Night City[/h2]

The anime is legendary too, and I love how they made sure that the Night City shown in the anime is the [b]exact[/b] same one we see in the game.

[hr][/hr]

[h2]Final thoughts[/h2]

This game can change the way you see certain things. It is one of the leading titles in the cyberpunk genre, and one of the few games that truly made me feel like I was inside the character.

Also, very few bugs remain after the 2.0 update and the following patches. In my third playthrough, where I completed 100% of the game, I experienced fewer than 10 bugs in over 100 hours.

In terms of performance and bugs, it is a [b]completely different game[/b] compared to launch.

This is first time i write a serious review, so yeah... 👀
The game run on average 30-45 FPS on a potato laptop with Medium to High settings (Low Crowd Density), i'll say it's good performance wise if you are gaming on the budget like me. 

as for the game itself, i think i will give it 8/10,
PROS:
- It's fun to run around as Netrunner in Night City, but after several hours i kinda feel that the game is kinda too easy, maybe my decision making a Netrunner V is have a big role on it too.

- The world itself feels big and lively (even at low crowd settings),
 
- The story is good enough to keep me playing, especially Phantom Liberty DLC part, i think the Relic mechanic it introduce is really fun, and Tower Ending (without Spoiler) might be the best ending of all time in my opinion. I would say though, after you watch some in-game news on tv, you probably will be like me and think "wow, the world building in this game is kinda fun and chaotic".

- CDPR also have make mod support for the games, making it kinda easy to add mods, especially for stuff like tiny immersion fixes, you know, the unnecessary detail like making all in game ads fully animated or pettable cat (yes you can adopt cat and iguana in this game, weirdly enough, you can only pat the iguana, still, they are both cute though).

- also, the music are awesome, if you have watched Edgerunner on Netflix, you might already have taste a little bit of those juicy bangers. but let me tell you, most of the songs in this games are really good. Recently, i even start to make a habbit of listening to some of the songs while doing chores, especially Chippin In, Pon Pon Sh*t (Jhonny Silverhand version, it's easter egg, not in Radio) and Never Fade Away, they really make me forgot how exhausting doing daily laundry was. (FYI, most of the songs from anime are in Body Heat Radio btw, including that song).

- Easter eggs, lots of easter eggs, really fun. I kinda don't want to talk too much about this because it was really fun if you notice something you don't know, but just you know, there are a lot of eater egg in this game. (just so you know, Murkman is the best detective)

CONS:
-Even though most of annoying bug from first released was already been patched and fixed, there are still some minor bug or glitches in the game, Some like driving drift, texture pop-in (not as bad as first released) and that one time Jackie walk through lockers during the first mission are might tied to FPS and you machine, but stuff like auto drive feature driver recklesly or showing negative million numbers (it was without mod btw) and monowire doesn't glow at all sometimes are still small stuff that need fixes in my opinion. (also, i know Fream gen is bad since it will give you low latency, but so far, i can't use frame gen at all. It will crash on me when i open map, even when using the settings for RTX 30s and 20s, so i don't know what kind of stuff that will happened if you able to use it on RTX 20s or 30s).

- lack of consequences might be one factor that make the story can feel a bit monotone and less exciting for me. Like, don't get me wrong, some dialogue choice does lock you out from good reward, like when i miss my chance to get Jackie guns for free, but like what i just said, "for free", mean you don't really missing out much when you make a wrong choices, if you own the DLC you can just buy your best friend's gun from the vendor in Dogtown. pretty neat since it was your best friend's gun, you know something that supposed to be sentimental for his family and people close by :). And not only that, if you fail to save someone who will give you car if you save them, you can also just,,, buy the car. Yeah, no sh*t i actually kinda lost excitement when i find out i can just buy stuff i missed out, like, i might get that some stuff like buying car from someone being killed in street race kinda make sense, but buying your own best friend's gun? I don't think Mama Wells or Misty will appreciate me to buy item that so sentimental for them (it kinda make me feels like paying people to make myself a grave robber). And it's just about rewards, you can try to just never talk when the timer is on during dialogue, there are some exception that instant kill you, but most of them at least from my experience, you don't really need to do anything to resolve the situation. I know that CDPR doesn't advertise this game as RPG anymore since the only RPG you get from this game is only a character building game like most J-RPG, but i mean, even as action game, it kinda lost to GTA, i still remember how hard it was to make decision in GTA 4. Still, i appreciate that the game locked you out from the side mission if you fail to do it (even if you can just save scum it.

- on last thing that less  of annoying and more of nitpicking for me is that you can't save car you stole. I know we don't play as literal criminal and mor of a merc in need of help in this game, but that doesn't mean you can't be criminal in the game. I mean, since 2.0 update, they add wanted system, it was fun even though you can kinda cheese it with going to safe zone. Why not try making a mechanic where you can hack a system of the car and it to your collection? Like limited amount of stolen cars that can be save  to your cloud so you can call it whenever until it was blown up and make it so that stolen car can't be repaint using the crystalcoat because unlike El Capitan, when you break the system of the car you can restore the neat content inside it. Idk, having permanent different colors of the same cars kinda sound interesting and stealing car outside the quests feel kinda useless for me right now.

THINGS THAT I WANT IN THE FUTURE:
- more updates, even sometimes update can break the mods, some stuff does need to be fix imo
- more DLC, we will get Witche 3 DLC in 2026-2027 (probably), i don't think one DLC is enough for this game, Phantom Liberty was fun, but i hope it get more story contents, and i do think it deserve more, maybe stuff that connected to Project orion?
- This one is for Project Orion i think, in the next game i hope the mission will be much more harder, the choices have much more weight on it and at the start of the mission, you can get a choice to either hire a merc to help you or call you online friends to help you if they really do want to make add the online function, just for immersion, because doing the mission alone against crowded enemy that have so many chrome kinda feels weird, well, this one doesn't feel weird because of the plot, but i don't think the same plot can work twice (well, even if i given choice to go with people, i might go alone though since it's video game, lol)

Au moment où j'écris ces lignes, j'ai a mon actif 4 runs réparties sur 520h réparties sur 4 ans, à chaque fois avec une manière de jouer différente : netrunner expert au katana, puis Mantis + Sandevistan, puis impitoyable runner spé grillade par Mur Noir (ma pp, il a pas l'air comme ça mais pourtant), puis tank indestructible qui fait des lancers à 3 points avec des membres de la MaxTac pour son sport du matin. Bon au vu de mes builds si j'avais un truc à redire, c'est que l'équilibrage du jeu fait que à partir d'un certain stade d'implants on délaisse les armes à feu (qui sont pourtant tout aussi belles, bien animées et bruitées les unes que les autres) car c'est NOUS, l'arme, et qu'on roule sur tout le monde même en difficulté max (mais qu'est ce que c'est jouissiiiiif).

Eh bien je n'ai jamais cessé d'être émerveillé par cet immense personnage qu'est Night City toute entière, impliqué dans tout ce que je faisais (du simple petit contrat à la méga mission d'espionnage) et dans les histoires proposées, et leurs personnages tous plus réussis et humains les uns que les autres, malgré leur chrome (sans pour autant être attaché émotionnellement comme pas mal de joueurs, je garde à l'esprit que ces des persos fictifs dans un jv, mais j'apprécie énormément leur écriture). Alors, on se retrouvera, à l'Afterlife !

Cyberpunk 2077 is one of the most rewarding gaming experiences I’ve ever had.

I’ve been here since launch, and yeah, the beginning was rough. Bugs, performance issues, missing features, it deserved the criticism it got. But even back then, I could see the vision CD Projekt Red was aiming for, and I stuck with it because there was something special underneath all the chaos.

Then 2.0 and Phantom Liberty dropped, and I completely fell in love with the game all over again.

Phantom Liberty felt like a Bond movie thrown into the violent world of 2077. The espionage, the characters, the music, the tension is incredible. Combined with the massive gameplay improvements from 2.0, the game finally became what I always hoped it could be. Especially since 2.0 came out after the disappointment that was Starfield.

Night City is honestly one of the best worlds ever created in gaming. It’s visually stunning, dense, atmospheric, and a dystopian outlook on the future that feels like what were heading towards IRL. I constantly found myself getting lost in the streets, neon lights, and just trying to explore every hidden nook and cranny. I just couldn’t get enough of this game.

For me, Cyberpunk 2077 is a 10/10 game that, like a phoenix rose from the ashes, and is now one of my all-time favorite RPGs. The 100% grind is fun and rewarding with none of it feels like too much of a grind. If you haven't played it yet, please do.
