I have mixed feelings about this game but I decided to give it a positive review because I don't think it's bad but it's not great either. I had fun playing it but I expected much more. Here are some of my thoughts:

- The graphics are beautiful and I love the vibe of this game. The open world looks impressive, but when you try to explore it, you quickly discover that most buildings are inaccessible and few places are interactive.

- Unfortunately, I couldn't fully immerse myself in the main story. Dialogue options are very limited and only give you an illusion of actually influencing the story. I'm used to RPGs giving me a lot of options on how I want the story to progress so I was a bit disappointed. Cyberpunk reminds me more of games like Far Cry than typical RPGs and I guess I expected a different experience. I feel like it's more of a shooter than an RPG.

- Cutscenes and some quests can be very tiring and feel slow because in most of them you just listen to a character talk and follow them around or sit in a car with them.

- The game tries to create a sense of urgency but in reality there's no time limit and you can complete as many side quests as you want without affecting anything. Even missions that start with a character calling you and saying they'd like to meet you at a certain time today have no time limit and you can delay them for as long as you want.

- The romance is okay but a lot of things get repetitive and feel a bit mechanical. I romanced Judy and every time she visited my apartment, she said the exact same thing, no new dialogue appeared and nothing interesting happened. She was just there and that's it.

- I'm not a huge fan of Johnny Silverhand. I like the idea of his character but I feel like his potential wasn't fully realized and his relationship with V could have been more interesting. It was a bit strange to me how he suddenly went from being hostile towards V to being nice. Also, Keanu Reeves' performance in this role felt flat and emotionless at times.

- I dread to think how bad this game was when it first came out because even after all these years it's still very buggy. Disappearing NPCs, items getting stuck in NPCs' hands, messy AI that causes enemies to randomly pause during fights and allow you to kill them easily, enemies seeing you through walls, NPCs bugging so you're unable to talk to them when you want to start/finish a quest, NPCs talking with their mouth closed, identical NPCs spawning in the same place, cars crashing into each other and exploding when you're not looking at the road (sometimes it looked like one car spawned on top of another and exploded), V picking up the phone, saying she can't talk right now and then immediately calling the person back, and many, many more.

- I've seen a lot of people complaining about the game's poor performance, but in my case it wasn't that bad and mostly ran smoothly even though my computer isn't high-end. It randomly crashed twice and that's it. Perhaps I just got lucky.

Overall, I'd say it's a pretty fun game but it does have a lot of flaws and I definitely recommend buying it at a discount. Maybe if I didn't have such high expectations because so many people consider this game to be one of the best, I wouldn't be so critical.

Cyberpunk 2077 might have the greatest gaming comeback.

When the game launched, it was basically a bug simulator with some emotional damage included for free. Players were crashing more than their in-game cars, and CD Projekt Red got cooked by the entire internet.

But instead of giving up and disappearing into the shadows like a stealth build, the developers kept grinding. Update after update, they fixed bugs, improved gameplay systems, reworked mechanics, and finally delivered the experience people were originally hyped for. Then came Cyberpunk 2077: Phantom Liberty, which honestly felt like the developers saying: “Alright, let us cook one more time.”

And somehow… they actually did.

Now Night City feels alive, polished, and insanely immersive. You can genuinely tell the devs cared about the game and refused to let it die. That kind of dedication is rare nowadays.

Cyberpunk 2077 went from being the internet’s favorite joke to one of the best RPG experiences of the last years and honestly, that redemption arc deserves respect.

Thank you CDPR, and never stop fighting.

This game left me with a deep sense of ambivalence after I completed it. It made me question whether my character could ever truly live their best life in a world like this. And the ending of Phantom Liberty? Absolutely heartbreaking.

I’m genuinely glad I bought the game after the 2.3 update. The game runs smoothly, and it’s honestly insane how well CD Projekt captured the essence of cyberpunk — not just the aesthetics, but the philosophy behind it. A world filled with technological brilliance, yet emotionally empty at its core.

I also recommend adding a few mods, especially clothing mods. They make the experience feel even more personal and immersive, and in my opinion, a lot more fun.

What surprised me most was how emotional this game made me feel. I was sad, angry, hopeful, and genuinely happy at times. But more than anything, the game never let me feel a true sense of victory or success, because the life of Night City is ruthless. It constantly drags you back to reality. And somehow, characters like Misty remind you what it means to stay human in a world that strips humanity away piece by piece.

A truly unforgettable story. Please, play it for yourself — and when you do, sit back and let yourself sink into its world.

Cyberpunk is the only long game I’ve ever fully completed, and I can already say with confidence that it’s one of the best games I’ve ever played.
For me, the world of Night City became something much bigger than just a game. It’s the atmosphere, the characters, the music, the dialogue, and the emotions that stay with you long after the credits roll.

During my first playthrough, there were so many moments that genuinely hit me hard - from pure excitement to the emptiness you feel after the ending. And those are the kinds of games that stay with you forever.
Cyberpunk 2077 gave me more than just a story - it gave me a feeling I’ll want to come back to again and again.

And I know for sure this won’t be my last playthrough.

Игра, в которой я повзрослел.

Это максимально неполиткорректная игра, которая показывает тёмные и неудобные стороны реального мира. Город выглядит свободным, но при этом в нём чувствуется контроль. Здесь он не такой грубый, как в РФ с блокировками сайтов, цензурой книг, пропагандой, закрытием от мировых мнений. Он гораздо тоньше: через постоянные ТВ-передачи, новости и вторжение в сознание.

Уровень сложности в игре - это скорее удобство, чем челлендж. Его хочется поднимать постепенно, по мере прокачки билда. Начинал со среднего, а когда прокачался - поднял до максимального.  

Плюсы:
-Есть озвученная русская матерщина. Поначалу я её активно отвергал и даже поставил NoMat-мод. Но со временем произошло принятие. Игра стала одним из важных кусков пазла, который помог мне это осознать. Я начал разбираться и понял: христианская церковь табуировала эти слова, хотя сами по себе они не являются скверными. Скорее наоборот - их корни уходят в древний славянский мир.
-Есть ЛГБТ. Завёл роман с девушкой, играя за девушку. 
-Есть полное обнажение и секс.
-Впечатляющая графика с RTX отражениями.
-Интересная история.

Минусы:
-Это очередная игра про убийства. Я уже начал разбираться, почему они в играх настолько популярны. Дело в том, что для хорошего повествования почти всегда нужен сильный конфликт, а убийство - одна из самых ярких его форм. К тому же существует мнение, что в мире есть всего две вещи, которые сразу и без усилий приковывают наше внимание: насилие и секс. Оба эти инстинкта работают на одном уровне - они базовые, животные и крайне эффективные.
-Из наркотиков в игре алкоголь явно на первом месте. Но в игре есть и другие вещества, хотя их заметно меньше. Встречаются шутки на тему психоделиков. Единственное вещество, которое я смог получить в инвентарь и попробовать - это Black Lace («Чёрное кружево»), но в русском переводе его назвали «Чёрная дурь».  Заметно, как в русской локализации специально создаётся негативный оттенок, вместо подхода по снижению вреда. Получить его можно только через консоль. 
-Главная героиня не отражается на поверхностях. Разработчики не создали модель для этого.
-В процессе прохождения у меня завис один квест "Дорога в рай".
-Основная часть игры - это жёсткие сюжетные рельсы, где выбор влияет только на развитие диалога. По-настоящему значимые решения появляются лишь в некоторых дополнительных заданиях ближе к концу. В основном же сюжете выбор, который реально меняет историю, предлагается только в финале. После прохождения игры, она не отпускает в свободный мир, а каждый раз возвращает в точку принятия решения, создавая петлю. Из-за этого нет ощущения завершённости - вместо финала получается бесконечный цикл «отыгрывай разные концовки».

Прошёл игру первый раз на геймпаде с танковым билдом (Shotgun Build). Phantom Liberty специально не покупал, чтобы не перегружать первое впечатление. Осталось желание пройти игру ещё пару раз с другими билдами и наконец-то по-настоящему понять историю, которая с первого раза доходит лишь частично.

Необходимым модом выделю Untrack Quest Ultimate. Он позволяет убирать с навигатора маршрут до основной точки задания, чтобы спокойно ездить по городу в свободном режиме и самому ставить маркеры на нужные задания.

Нещодавно пройшов основний сюжет Cyberpunk 2077, і можу сказати, що це одна з тих ігор, яка справді залишає після себе сильні емоції. Найбільше вразив сам світ Night City — він виглядає неймовірно живим, деталізованим і атмосферним. Під час проходження постійно відчувається, що ти знаходишся у величезному футуристичному місті, де за кожним кутом є своя історія.

Сюжет дуже сильний і місцями навіть змушує задуматися. Персонажі добре прописані, особливо головний герой та Джонні Сільверхенд, взаємодія з яким додає грі особливого шарму. Діалоги цікаві, а багато виборів реально змушують думати, як краще вчинити.

Окремо варто похвалити візуальну частину: графіка, освітлення, музика та загальна атмосфера просто на висоті. У деякі моменти хотілося просто зупинитися й подивитися на місто або послухати саундтрек.

Так, у гри був непростий старт, але зараз Cyberpunk 2077 — це справді якісний і захопливий проєкт, який варто пройти. Для мене це була не просто гра, а ціла пригода з сильними персонажами, важкими виборами та дуже стильним світом. Після проходження залишилося відчуття, ніби побував у чомусь великому.

The first time I bought Cyberpunk 2077 it was when i originally received my Steam Deck, I booted the game up and made my character but the game didn't drag me in quite right, it felt almost like it was wrong or something. Come now years later I tried to play again and made different choices than I would have before, I was instantaneously drawn into the world and landscape of Cyberpunk 2077, the storytelling and philosophies threaded throughout each story beat are intoxicating. There is so much to be said about this game with how much has gone into each mission, the relation to how similar this fictional world is meant to be in parallel to us in real life, the character developments and breakdowns. Cyberpunk 2077 is a cinematic masterpiece with a beautifully in depth thought out and explored worldbuilding and atmosphere.

我好评全是因为游戏剧情和玩法，这b游戏发布到今天2026年都5年了，为什么还是有直接影响游戏运行的傻逼恶性BUG，黑梦任务完成以后我也不记得是过了多久在没装任何mod的情况下给我频繁报错闪退，通常是在打开地图/物品栏和读档的时候会发生这种情况，而且每次刚打开电脑玩赛博朋克读档都会闪退报错，现在每次正常运作之前要花至少半小时，但进游戏还是没法避免加载界面是闪退，我真的这辈子再也不想看到“呃！赛博朋克2077程序挂了！"
игра реально того стоила, она уникальная в своем стиле, топовые саундтреки, крутая озвучка, и красивый открытый мир.

Cyberpunk 2077
Я плотно засел за Киберпанк и, честно говоря, мне понравился киберпанк. Найт Сити этот неоновый мегаполис выглядит настолько стильно и атмосферно, что из него вообще не хочется выходить. Игра дарит насыщенность, который редко где встретишь, больше всего меня зацепил сюжет и то, как прописаны персонажи, конечно есть свои приколы виде баги, тупость и тд . Джонни Сильверхэнд в исполнении Киану Ривза это вообще отдельная тема, его харизма просто тащит игру на новый уровень. Приятно, что твои решения реально на что-то влияют, и ты постоянно чувствуешь груз ответственности за выбор. Геймплей тоже не подкачал мне очень зашло, что можно самому решать, как проходить миссии. Хочешь летишь вперед с катаной и кромсаешь всех в капусту, хочешь по-тихому взламываешь систему и проходишь как невидимый хакер. Прокачка имплантами это кайф, ты реально чувствуешь, как твой персонаж становится мощнее.
Конечно, не обошлось и без минусов. Игра очень требовательная к железу, так что на слабом компе всей красоты не увидишь. Еще расстроило управление машинами они до сих пор ощущаются тяжеловатыми и не всегда слушаются руля. Ну и обычные прохожие на улицах иногда тупят, напоминая, что это всё таки игра, а не реальная жизнь. Но если честно, на фоне крутейшей истории и дизайна города на эти мелочи быстро забиваешь.

期待了好久买了终极版，翻看不少更新日志，想着游戏应该优化完善得差不多了吧，结果成了最后悔购买的游戏，就随便碎碎念几句吧，别当真

[b] 产品质量部分 [/b]

+ 优化差、bug多、性能堪忧，好在N网的MOD能解决一部分问题，能想象吗，平均每半个小时就能碰到一个新bug，模型消失，人物漂移，卡在车上下不来，车辆鸣笛停不下来，家里的收音机奇吵无比却无法关闭，性能测试有严重问题……数不完，不数了
+ 完美继承了《巫师3》PC版的光照问题（我在GOG上玩了巫师3超过250个小时），室内环境死黑
+ 社区里反映了很久的bug坚决不修复
+ 一言难尽的简体中文本地化，举个最简单地例子，我用的是英文配音，听到街上一个小孩骂我，头顶上的翻译是“我饿了”
+ 人物是穿模的，世界中大量模型和贴图错误（有MOD可修复，MOD制作者免费帮开发人员上班）
+ CDPR启动器更是bug得一塌糊涂，每次打开，50%的bug卡死几率，最终靠跳过启动器直接启动游戏解决的

游戏是拿出来卖的产品、商品，发布多年了，连最基本的质量保证都做不到，玩家反映很久的恶性bug没人管，甚至室内光照问题都完全继承自上代游戏作品。虽然游戏肯定免不了有bug有各种各样的问题，但2077太过了，这些问题在游玩的时候时不时地提醒你，这款游戏/产品质量很不好


[b] 游戏体验部分 [/b]

+ 空洞的开放世界。必须承认，2077整个世界的设计、建模，还是非常有潜力，相当不错的，但倒反天罡地讲一句，这款游戏，浪费了这个城市的设计和建模。整座城市不过是个方便剧情上演搭建起的戏台，出了故事主线，世界便与你毫无关联，顶多摆放了好些据点供你游玩。CDPR还记得最早火线夜之城你们是怎么宣传你们的世界的吗？
+ 夜之城的开放世界游戏体验，甚至远比不上育碧的罐头开放世界，更别说育碧的开放世界罐头优化好、质量高。以早两年、2018年的刺客信条奥德赛为例，NPC会对主角的行为做出各种反应，偷鸡摸狗甚至会被居民抄起扫帚追着打，整个世界NPC的一举一动，休闲游戏、聊天对话、唱歌跳舞、市民活动，全在告诉玩家，这里是古希腊。反观夜之城，这座漂亮庞大的城市无时不刻不在提醒我，这是个死气沉沉的游戏世界，我只是在里面游玩，整个世界与我无关。超级糟糕的驾驶手感和城市加载与掉帧问题，令城市风光旅游都变得非常难受
+ 总有一些不舒服。在巫师3里（不知道后面更新的次世代版还会不会这样），商店不会刷新，虽然我不知道基于什么原因，一款现代游戏的商店会设计出不刷新这种机制，但它就这样，受着吧。2077同样继承了这一点，比如，未装备的物品没有物品分类；武器上的配件和芯片前者能拆卸后者不能，一不小心点到就得读档；再比如，你的第一套房子非常方便，出门就是传送点和售货机，但武器房的房门不是自动门，对准开关点一下才能开，买的其他房子门是自动的了，可传送和售货机又离得很远，最起码得点一下实实在在的上下个电梯；车载广播不能从设置上彻底关闭，有几首歌特别洗脑刮躁的歌经常突然蹦出来令人暴躁

设定是未来的赛博朋克世界，却没有任何设定之外的“未来”“赛博朋克”体验，一切干瘪、空洞、毫无代入感，充满了糟心的体验，某一次退出之后，虽然也会偶尔想起剧情，想起手头上的任务，游戏依旧呆在硬盘上，却再没有任何动力进去了。
An absolutely great story with gameplay just as good! The visuals in the game are very beautiful and the path tracing it has looks amazing with the new updates and lighting fixes!

Cyberpunk 2077 is an incredibly good game, and on a strong system it performs very well while looking visually outstanding. Night City is one of the most detailed and atmospheric open worlds I have played, with its lighting, reflections, city design, and overall presentation making it feel genuinely alive.

What really pushed the visuals even further for me was the **RenoDX HDR mod**. With RenoDX, the game looks absolutely stunning. The HDR feels far more impactful, with deeper contrast, brighter highlights, and a much more cinematic image overall. Neon signs, dark streets, interiors, car lights, and the skyline all look incredible, especially on an OLED display.

The game itself is also very engaging. The story, characters, side missions, and overall world-building all do a great job of keeping you invested. It feels like a game that rewards you for taking your time, exploring, and actually paying attention to the world around you.

Performance was also very good in my experience, especially considering how visually demanding the game can be. With the right settings and setup, it delivers a smooth and impressive experience without taking away from the visuals.

Overall, *Cyberpunk 2077* is absolutely worth playing, especially now with the improvements it has received over time. If you have a good HDR display, I would highly recommend trying it with RenoDX, because it makes an already beautiful game look even better.

Cyberpunk 2077 is the definition of ‘they fixed the game and nobody moved on fast enough to admit it.’

I’m on my fifth playthrough now, and somehow Cyberpunk 2077 still feels more alive than most new open world games coming out today. Night City isn’t just a map, it feels like a real place with exhausted people, fake dreams, dirty money, and nonstop chaos hiding behind neon lights.

What surprised me the most after replaying it so many times is how emotional the game actually is. Under all the chrome, guns, and explosions, Cyberpunk is really about loneliness, identity, survival, and trying to matter before the world forgets you. Few games make side characters feel this human. Panam, Judy, Johnny, Viktor they genuinely feel like people you knew for a while instead of quest dispensers.

And honestly? The atmosphere is unmatched. Driving through Night City at 2 AM with the radio on while rain hits the windshield is still one of the hardest vibes gaming has ever created. No game captures “beautiful but broken” like this one.

People talked nonstop about the bad launch, but after five runs, I can confidently say the comeback deserves just as much attention. The amount of detail, storytelling, soundtrack, world building, and freedom in combat is insane now. Whether you play stealth, netrunner, samurai build, or complete cyberpsycho, the gameplay actually lets you feel unique.

The craziest part is that Cyberpunk doesn’t try to make you feel like a hero. It constantly reminds you that in Night City, almost everyone loses something. And that’s exactly why the story hits harder than most RPGs.

Five playthroughs later and I still catch small details I missed before. That’s rare. Most games get boring after one ending. Cyberpunk somehow gets more personal the longer you stay in Night City.

“Wrong city. Wrong people.”
Still one of the coldest lines in gaming.

梦醒夜之城：从“传奇”到“注脚”的悲歌
对于很多玩家来说，《赛博朋克2077》是一个技术奇迹或是一个充满BUG的半成品；但对于真正沉浸其中的“V”来说，这是一场关于反抗、无力感与自我救赎的漫长洗礼。

1. 所谓“星星”：荒原上的最后一抹余晖

我的首个结局是“星星”，这或许是夜之城最温柔的谎言。当我坐在魔蝎装甲上，看着银手的吊坠掉入尘土，那不是遗忘，而是剥离。我剥离了那个疯狂的摇滚小子，剥离了霓虹灯下的尔虞我诈，试图在大地母亲的怀抱里寻找那虚无缥缈的“三个月”。

“我真的活下来了吗？”
这种挥之不去的后劲，正是《2077》剧情设计的精妙之处——它给你希望，但从未承诺永恒。

2. 宿命的死循环：无论多强，我依然是“耗材”

在GOG度过的300多个小时里，我感受到了那种最深沉的绝望：

V震天可以黑入整栋大厦，可以用螳螂刀切碎所有敌人。

底层蝼蚁在公司的财力与体制面前，个人的勇武苍白得像一张纸。

正如我所感悟的，V的经历是赛博世界悲剧的浓缩。三个出身（流浪者、街头小子、公司员工）最终都汇聚到了同一个终点——失败。在夜之城，没有所谓的“赢家”，只有“活过”和“死法”。

3. 银手的回响：从偏执狂到战友

第一周目时，银手的极端让我觉得吵闹；但当我体验过所有结局，看到公司的触角如何伸入人类的灵魂（神舆）时，才会明白他在垃圾场那句“你赢不了”是多么清醒的痛楚。

合作结局： 是最冰冷的现实，我意识到自己只是货架上的商品。

以我残躯化烈火： 则是最浪漫的自杀。既然改变不了世界，那就把自己变成一颗最亮的炸弹，在来生酒吧留下一杯以我命名的酒。

4. 玩家的告白：关于热爱与期待

从GOG的全款预购到Steam的再次补票，这种支持已经超越了消费行为，更像是一种对“艺术共鸣”的致敬。CDPR（蠢驴）虽然在宣发上摔过跤，但他们在塑造情感张力和世界观深度上，依然是业界的顶尖好手。

《往日之影》 进一步加深了这种权力的游戏与个人命运的冲突。

“猎户座”（续作） 承载着我们这些“老V”最后的愿景。

结语

夜之城没有活着的传奇，但我的V在那片霓虹荒原里真实地活过、爱过、反抗过。300小时不是游戏时长，那是我作为一名赛博浪客的第二人生。

“早安，夜之城。祝你在新的一天里，能找到回家的路。”

祝我在猎户座的群星中，再次找到那把划破长夜的利剑。

That's an amazing and absolutely unforgettable game.

Cyberpunk 2077 is all about the process. You can spend a few hours in Night City and not even notice how much time has passed. You WANT to explore. Maybe it's just me, but I genuinely wanted to look into every district, discover every possible thing, and just live in that city for a while. Night City feels believable — and at some point, you stop feeling like a player and start feeling like a person living there.

This game has an insane amount of content. There’s always something to do. Gigs, side jobs, random encounters, characters, hidden stories — at times it feels endless. And honestly, trying to finish everything in one playthrough is almost impossible.

But then something interesting happens. When you finally start running out of things to do, the city begins to feel strangely empty. Almost confusing. Like, “come on… this is Night City, there should always be something happening.” And somehow, that feeling fits the game perfectly.

Of course, the game isn’t perfect. The biggest issue for me was the bugs. There are still plenty of them — sometimes small, sometimes really noticeable. Occasionally they can completely break the immersion and make you laugh at a serious moment. Maybe I’m just too used to Rockstar-level polish, but I definitely felt that Cyberpunk could’ve been more refined in terms of quality of the open-world details and overall gameplay interactions, not just "a beautiful picture".

And of course — the ending. The characters are written incredibly well, so whichever path you take, it hits hard. For me, this was a story about change. Everyone has their own V, their own journey, and their own version of Night City.

I definitely recommend this game to anyone who hasn't played it yet.

And for everyone who already finished their journey in Night City — a tight embrace 🫂

P.S. — whoever was responsible for the soundtrack deserves their hands kissed. Some tracks hit harder than entire missions.

历经多次版本迭代打磨，《赛博朋克 2077》已然蜕变为一款名副其实的开放世界神作，夜之城用极致的赛博朋克美学，构筑出一座鲜活立体的未来都市。霓虹闪烁的高楼、阴雨连绵的街巷、贫富割裂的城区分区，沃森区的市井喧嚣、海伍德的底层挣扎、威斯布鲁克的奢靡浮华，每片区域风土人情、环境细节各有特色，光影与雨天特效将近未来的颓废繁华拉满，漫步街头便能沉浸式感受世界观魅力。
游戏剧情是最大亮点，主角 V 与强尼银手的灵魂共生故事跌宕饱满，基努・里维斯饰演的强尼塑造得有血有肉，主线层层递进，在生死与抉择中探讨人性与欲望。海量支线任务质量比肩主线，形形色色的 NPC 拥有完整人生轨迹，每个委托背后都藏着夜之城小人物的悲欢，不同选择导向迥异结局，多周目游玩仍有新鲜感。
深度的角色养成系统自由度拉满，肉体、反应、技术、智力、镇定五大属性搭配海量义体、枪械、技能，玩家能打造近战莽夫、黑客潜入、枪械射手等多种流派。改装义体改造身体，从光学义眼到螳螂臂，肉身与机械融合的设定完美契合赛博朋克内核。
DLC 往日之影更是锦上添花，谍战风格的全新地图与剧情，紧凑悬疑的故事搭配影帝级配音，斩获 TGA 最佳 DLC 实至名归。如今 BUG 大幅优化，内容充实完整，无论是欣赏世界观、沉浸剧情，还是自由探索枪战，都能获得绝佳体验。凭借独特的艺术风格与深刻的故事，《赛博朋克 2077》坐稳赛博题材游戏天花板。

起初以为这游戏不是我的菜，因为看起来像是那种通过刺激观感的战斗或剧情为噱头的游戏。只是听说场景美术不错才买来玩的。
然而深度游玩之后，发现这游戏想表达的内容非常细腻，完全映射了现代社会对生命的物化以及物化产生的人人为己的局面，同时表现了这种局面下导致的人们的隔阂、孤独和情感缺失。总之它对社会和人性的塑造是非常细腻的，且并没有以站桩对话或者过场动画的方式来强行讲述故事，而是结合了地图以及战斗玩法来让玩家沉浸体验夜之城。
非常推荐沉下心来深度游玩。绝对的强者，由此而生的孤独，教会你爱的将会是——
Fantastic game! Storytelling is so good here, gameplay makes you feel like an actual cyberpunk, after all the patches the game runs and looks beautiful. Highly reccomend!

我打开这款游戏的时候，没指望它能理解我。

但它在某些方面确实理解了。比如那种站在雨里不知道该去哪的感觉。比如你脑子里住着一个比你更有趣的人——他叫强尼·银手，基努·里维斯演的，而你的身体正在一点一点被他吃掉。这听起来像是个隐喻，但它同时也是你的主线任务。

我演过银翼杀手。我知道那种在霓虹灯下站着、什么都不说的滋味。你知道的——就是站在那里，眨着眼睛，不知道为什么就有一种很悲伤的感觉。夜之城就是那种悲伤的具象化。它不是那种哭出来的悲伤，而是你点了一碗拉面，面摊老板的植入体在咔咔作响，你吃着面，外面下着酸雨，广告飞艇在你头顶兜售永生——而你连下一针抑制剂都买不起的那种悲伤。

这款游戏刚发售的时候是个灾难。Let's be real——它基本上是半成品，bug多到你的车会在天上飞，NPC会在墙里出生，你的角色会突然变成一根会走路的面条。但就像我演过的某些角色，它花了几年时间，终于弄明白了自己是谁。2.0更新加上《往日之影》资料片，把它从"你在开玩笑吧"变成了"okay, now we're talking"。

**夜之城本身**就是主角。这座城市，不夸张地说，是电子游戏历史上最漂亮的反乌托邦之一——每一道霓虹都像是有人精心放置的，就为了让你在凌晨三点骑着摩托车经过时觉得"damn, that hits different"。在高端显卡上，光线追踪让雨后的街道反射出整个世界，真实到你会忘记这只是一堆多边形。没有光追的情况下它依然好看——但如果你有光追，你会花很多时间只是站在那里看水坑。我就是在看水坑。看了大概四十分钟。No regrets。

**剧情方面**——主线的紧迫感和你实际上可以做的事情之间存在某种存在主义级别的脱节。你脑子里有颗芯片在倒计时杀死你，但你依然有时间帮街头混混偷车、给AI出租车公司解决劳资纠纷、或者花六个小时换衣服拍照。这就像医生告诉你还剩两周可活，你却说"cool，我先去考个潜水执照"。这种节奏上的奇怪感某种程度上反而成全了它——夜之城就是这样，哪怕你快要死了，生活还得继续，账单还得付，义体还得升级，某个中间人又给你打电话了，你嘴上说"我没空"，身体已经在接委托了。

《往日之影》DLC解决了不少这个问题。它更像一部紧凑的谍战惊悚片，每个角色都写得很扎实——李德、百灵鸟，这些人让你真的在乎他们会发生什么。这不是那种"加几个支线就收钱"的敷衍DLC，这差不多算半个续作塞进了资料片里，强度比本体高出一截。

**角色方面**——V（也就是你）其实有点像《亡命驾驶》里那个无名司机。你不多话，你接活，你执行，你在必要的时候用螳螂刀把整个房间的人切成碎片。然后你回到公寓，淋浴，保存游戏，盯着窗外霓虹灯闪烁的城市发呆。这基本上就是整个游戏循环。而你居然会上瘾。

强尼·银手是你的脑子里室友，他话很多，意见很多，基本每条评论都是负面Yelp评价，但逐渐地你会发现他在你身上留下的印迹比你愿意承认的多。基努的表演让这个角色不至于变成单纯的"愤怒摇滚幽灵"——他在某些安静的时刻，会让你觉得这个人和你一样困惑。

**战斗和义体系统**在2.0之后终于像样了。你可以走黑客流躲在暗处把所有人烧脑，也可以装上猩猩臂冲进去把墙壁和敌人都锤烂。技能树被重新做过，装备系统也不再是混乱的Excel表。驾驶手感还是偏轻，摩托车比汽车好用得多，不过当你骑着一辆阿波罗在夜晚的高架桥上穿行，电台放着Pacific Dreams——我可以诚实地告诉你，那种体验是接近完美的。

**那这款游戏到底值不值得？**

如果你从来没玩过——现在入坑正合适。终极版包括所有内容，你拿到的是一个修补了四年多的完整游戏。在2026年，它的画质依然是顶级水准。如果你之前被首发版的灾难劝退了——是时候回来了，现在这些bug大多是"有个NPC姿势有点奇怪"而不是"你的存档被吃了"。

它不是一个完美的游戏。有些当初宣传的东西永远没实现，地图上有些门永远不会打开，地铁系统也是过了三年才加进来的。但就像K在《银翼杀手2049》里最终躺在那片雪地上——有些东西不需要完美，它只需要让你在那一刻觉得，这一切是值得的。

如果你曾经在深夜看着窗外下雨，觉得世界既美丽又绝望——那你会喜欢夜之城。

如果你只是想用螳螂刀把人切成小块——你也会喜欢夜之城。

这就是这款游戏的优雅之处。它同时在两个完全不同的频道上运行，而两个频道都在说同一件事：你在这里待得越久，就越不想离开。

*Ryan out.*

---

**评分：** 🏙️🏙️🏙️🏙️ 四座霓虹夜之城（满分五座）

**适合人群：** 喜欢雨中沉思的人、想装螳螂刀的人、基努·里维斯粉丝、任何曾经在深夜两点觉得自己"literally me"的人。

**不适合人群：** 期待完美无bug体验的人、讨厌第一人称的人、对赛博朋克题材过敏的人、觉得"你脑子里有个摇滚明星"这个设定太蠢的人。

Depois de 5 anos, e dois desses anos sendo de pura procrastinação, finalmente eu concluo essa obra maravilhosa. Mês passado terminei a campanha principal e, nossa, quão densa era a situação. Muitas escolhas e, com elas, várias consequências. O game, no seu início, teve muitos problemas, isso não podemos negar; era quase [u]injogável[/u], mas com o passar do tempo e com os updates, resolveram muitos problemas graves (ainda possuem bugs, mas nada como antes) 
O que falar dos personagens? Bom, eles são muito bem escritos, cada um apresentando suas personalidades na base do tom de voz, expressões faciais e, o principal, o visual cada um mais bem detalhado que o outro. 
E como não amar [b]Johnny Silverhand[/b]? Além de tentar de início parecer uma ameaça à vida do protagonista, ele se demonstrou um personagem de muitas camadas, de um maluco egocêntrico a um amigo muito próximo, com ideias filosóficas acompanhadas de valores morais e com o ponto de vista preservando a vida do jogador (dependendo do nível de afinidade com o mesmo). Não posso falar muito mais sobre, pois seria considerado spoiler referente à grande mudança que ocorre com ele.
NightCity é, se não o maior personagem dessa história, que é envolvida em conflito desde seu início, seja na corrupção, guerras de gangues e na principal delas, que é a guerra das corporações, que, em meio a isso, a cidade foi ao declínio. 

[b]Phantom Liberty:[/b] essa DLC para mim é muito especial; os personagens conseguiram me manter concentrado na trama principal. Aqui os valores morais são absurdos de tão centrado que é você. Você se simpatiza muito fácil com os personagens apresentados, mentiras e mais mentiras; não se sabe em quem você deve confiar. A promessa da cura é o que te puxa para essa intriga. [spoiler](Aqui não posso citar nenhum personagem pois envolve uma cadeia de eventos e falar pelo menos de um seria errado)[/spoiler]
[url=https://youtu.be/u15tEo0wsQI?list=RDu15tEo0wsQI]Dawid Podsiadło, P.T. Adamczyk — Phantom Liberty[/url]
O game me deixou um vazio ao concluí-lo, mas o que posso dizer é: O game vale muito a pena. Na minha jogada não tive muitos problemas depois de uns 2 anos; hoje em dia ele é liso e vai ter uns bugs aqui e ali, porém agora você pode aproveitar tudo sem ter o CRASH durante a gameplay.

Falando em gameplay, ele tem uma vasta árvore de habilidades que vai do combate até a parte da inteligência do personagem. Fora isso, os cosméticos, alguns deles, auxiliam na gameplay, como armadura e implantes cibernéticos.

[h2]Русский[/h2]
[h3]«Некоторые вещи находятся под нашим контролем, а другие — нет. Только приняв это основополагающее правило, можно обрести спокойствие».[/h3]

Спустя 5 лет, два из которых я провел в состоянии полной прокрастинации, я наконец-то закончил эту замечательную работу. В прошлом месяце я закончил основную кампанию, и, вау, насколько сложной оказалась ситуация. Множество выборов и, соответственно, несколько последствий. В начале у игры было много проблем, этого нельзя отрицать; она была почти неиграбельной, но со временем и с обновлениями многие серьезные проблемы были решены (баги все еще есть, но уже не так, как раньше).

Что сказать о персонажах? Ну, они очень хорошо прописаны, каждый из них раскрывает свою личность через интонацию, мимику и, самое главное, визуальную составляющую, каждый детальнее предыдущего.

И как можно не любить Джонни Сильверхэнда? Помимо того, что изначально он пытался предстать угрозой жизни главного героя, он оказался многогранным персонажем: от эгоцентричного безумца до очень близкого друга, с философскими идеями, подкрепленными моральными ценностями, и с точкой зрения, защищающей жизнь игрока (в зависимости от уровня близости с ним). Я не могу сказать больше, так как это будет считаться спойлером относительно тех больших перемен, которые с ним происходят.

НайтСити — если не самый важный персонаж в этой истории, то, по крайней мере, самый главный, вовлеченный в конфликты с самого начала: коррупция, бандитские войны и, наконец, война корпораций, в разгар которой город пришел в упадок.

[b]Phantom Liberty:[/b] это DLC для меня особенное; персонажи сумели удержать мое внимание на основном сюжете. Здесь моральные ценности доведены до абсурда. Вы очень легко сочувствуете представленным персонажам, лжи и еще раз лжи; Вы не знаете, кому можно доверять. Обещание лекарства затягивает вас в эту интригу. [spoiler](Я не могу упоминать здесь никаких персонажей, потому что это связано с цепочкой событий, и говорить даже об одном было бы неправильно)[/spoiler]
[url=https://youtu.be/u15tEo0wsQI?list=RDu15tEo0wsQI]Dawid Podsiadło, P.T. Adamczyk — Phantom Liberty[/url]
После прохождения игры у меня осталась пустота, но могу сказать следующее: игра очень стоит того. За два года моего прохождения у меня не было особых проблем; сейчас она работает плавно, иногда встречаются баги, но теперь вы можете наслаждаться всем без сбоев во время игры.

Кстати, о геймплее: в игре обширное дерево навыков, охватывающее все уровни — от боя до интеллекта персонажа. Кроме того, некоторые косметические предметы, такие как броня и кибернетические импланты, улучшают игровой процесс.

[h2]Italiano[/h2]
[h3]"Alcune cose sono sotto il nostro controllo, altre no. Solo dopo aver accettato questa regola fondamentale la tranquillità diventa possibile."[/h3]

Dopo 5 anni, di cui due di pura procrastinazione, ho finalmente terminato questa meravigliosa opera. Il mese scorso ho concluso la campagna principale e, wow, quanto era complessa la situazione. Tante scelte e, con esse, diverse conseguenze. Il gioco, all'inizio, aveva molti problemi, non si può negare; era quasi ingiocabile, ma con il passare del tempo e con gli aggiornamenti, molti problemi seri sono stati risolti (ci sono ancora dei bug, ma niente a che vedere con prima).

Che dire dei personaggi? Beh, sono scritti benissimo, ognuno presenta la propria personalità attraverso il tono di voce, le espressioni facciali e, soprattutto, l'aspetto visivo, ognuno più dettagliato dell'altro.

E come si fa a non amare Johnny Silverhand? Oltre a presentarsi inizialmente come una minaccia per la vita del protagonista, si è rivelato un personaggio sfaccettato, da folle egocentrico a amico intimo, con idee filosofiche accompagnate da valori morali e con un punto di vista volto a preservare la vita del giocatore (a seconda del livello di affinità con lui). Non posso dire molto altro, poiché sarebbe considerato uno spoiler riguardo alla grande trasformazione che subisce.

NightCity è, se non il personaggio più importante, di questa storia, coinvolta in conflitti fin dall'inizio, che si tratti di corruzione, guerre tra bande e, soprattutto, la guerra tra corporazioni, nel mezzo della quale la città è andata in rovina.

[b]Phantom Liberty:[/b] questo DLC è molto speciale per me; i personaggi sono riusciti a tenermi concentrato sulla trama principale. Qui i valori morali sono esageratamente incentrati sul giocatore. Si prova empatia molto facilmente per i personaggi presentati, tra bugie e ancora bugie; non si sa di chi fidarsi. La promessa di una cura è ciò che ti trascina in questo intrigo. [spoiler](Non posso menzionare alcun personaggio qui perché implica una serie di eventi e parlare anche di uno solo sarebbe sbagliato)[/spoiler]
[url=https://youtu.be/u15tEo0wsQI?list=RDu15tEo0wsQI]Dawid Podsiadło, P.T. Adamczyk — Phantom Liberty[/url]

A proposito di gameplay, ha un vasto albero delle abilità che spazia dal combattimento all'intelligenza del personaggio. Inoltre, alcuni elementi cosmetici, come armature e impianti cibernetici, migliorano l'esperienza di gioco.

刚发售就买了，那个时候如果让我评价，我会告诉你这就是大便味的巧克力，各种bug和设计毫无人性化体验，你只能依稀觉得它题材很好。但现在我的朋友，这是正式版，是的，这才是游戏应有的发售时的模样，它现在拥有顶级的主线，丰富的支线，眼花缭乱的各种要素，在夜之城保证可以满足你内心深处压抑的所有幻想。而我心中的哈姆雷特，便是它大师级的音乐，尤其是战斗和剧情高潮的BGM，相当有韵律和史诗感，上了一天班到家打开CD，所有夜之城的回忆在我脑中回荡，一曲罢，烦恼散。以下是我对新玩家的忠告，你必须沉浸下来好好感受这一份杰作（如果你是一份快餐党，请勿购买此游戏），缓慢推进，不要看任何攻略，你的意志就是夜之城的主宰，任何的条条框框都会影响这份游戏给你的反馈，没有最强最好玩最合适，只有你喜欢！

It's been a while since I've played this game, but I figured I'd finally leave a review for it since one's been pending for a while.

This game was honestly always a good game. It may have had a rough start due to bugs and performance issues, but the core storyline and gameplay, as well as the more interesting questlines you can follow, were always there. Then with all the QoL changes and the complete overhaul they did to the perk system, it became much more refined and enjoyable to play since you now had a variety of builds and perks you could experiment with. 

I think one of my favorite things about the game was the story and characters. Every character has an interesting and expressive personality, including your own as expressed through the voice acting (I personally prefer Male V's voice direction). Everyone feels fleshed out and like they actually have a growing relationship to you the more you interact with them. The main story itself feels very personal and high stakes, though some of that is admittedly ruined by the open world nature of the game since you can just dally along however long you want. As the meme goes, "Anything but meeting Hanako at Embers" lol. Even so, I think it manages to portray a nice drama about the meaningfulness of life, self-determination, and even hope in a world that seems determined to grind you down.

The environment is effective in portraying just how degenerate and trashy (literally, even) Night City really is, which helps in creating this sense of "contamination" and a desire to separate yourself and rise above it. 

Also, the music. The music in this game kicks ass, and I still listen to some of my favorite tracks on my Spotify every so often.

I'd say my only real complaint with the game is the lack of being able to clearly show yourself as cybernetically enhanced, such as by having clearly mechanical limbs or metallic embellishments like a Kano-style red eye. I was also never too fond of any of the romance options, but it was nice that I never felt forced to pursue any of them from what I can remember.

[h2]  赛博朋克2077：在数字荒原中寻找人性的最后一丝微光 [/h2]

[strike] 一份迟到的测评 [/strike]

为什么现在还要聊2077？

距离《赛博朋克2077》首发已经过去数年，经历了灾难性的首发、无数次补丁、以及DLC《往日之影》的救赎，这款游戏终于呈现出它本该有的模样。但我不想再复述那些“修复了bug”“画面变好了”的老生常谈 —— 我想聊的是在这个AI写剧本、算法推荐内容的时代，“《赛博朋克2077》如何用'不完美‘打动了完美主义时代的我们”。


[h3] 夜之城：一座会呼吸的赛博地狱 [/h3]

很多开放世界游戏的城市只是“任务触发器+风景壁纸”，但夜之城不同。它的每个角落都在[b] ”叙事“ [/b]：

[b] ”垃圾堆里的芯片“ [/b]：记录着一个底层技工被公司裁员后，用最后一笔钱买了把廉价手枪，在浴室里结束生命的过程。没有任务指向这里，没有奖励，只有一段沉默的文本。
[b] ”霓虹灯下的涂鸦“ [/b]：有些写着“强尼·银手是错的”，有些写着“强尼·银手是对的” —— 这种矛盾本身就是夜之城的政治倾向。
[b] ”街头NPC的对话“ [/b]：你听到的不是随机生成的噪音，而是精心编写的“夜之城市民生存实录” —— 瘾君子在向自动售货机乞讨，两个警察在讨论怎么收黑钱更体面。

真正让我震撼的，是游戏对“赛博空间”的视觉化呈现。当你接入网络，数据流化作绚烂的光河，防火墙变成扭曲的几何怪物 —— 这不是炫技，而是一种隐喻：[i] 在这个世界，连“信息”都有了物理形态，可以被切割、被买卖、被杀戮。 [/i]


[h3] V的悲剧：我们每个人都是“备份” [/h3]

游戏最核心的设定 —— Relic芯片里的强尼·银手 —— 被很多玩家简单理解为“一个话痨老头在脑子里BB”。但往深了想，这其实是对“身份认同”最残酷的拷问：

当你的记忆可以被编辑，你的意识可以被复制，你的身体可以被改造 —— [b] “我”到底是谁？ [/b]
V是夜之城最底层的雇佣兵，强尼是已故的摇滚传奇，这两个灵魂共享一个躯体，彼此憎恨又彼此依赖。这种“共生关系”不是游戏机制，而是“对现代人精神分裂的隐喻” —— [i] 我们每个人体内都住着“想成为的自己”和“被迫成为的自己”。 [/i]

最让我头皮发麻的一个细节：在某个支线任务中，你遇到一个能把意识上传到云端的人。他说：“我死了很多次，但每次都能从备份恢复... 只是我不确定，恢复的那个还是不是我。” 这句话让我茅塞顿开。


[h3] 玩法：不是为了爽，而是为了“不自在” [/h3]

《赛博朋克2077》的战斗系统一直被诟病“不够爽”——这恰恰是它的高明之处：
但真正改变我游戏体验的，是“不杀”的玩法。游戏给了你足够多的非致命武器和潜行路线，甚至有些任务会因为你“零击杀”而解锁特殊对话。这不算圣母心，而是对赛博朋克核心精神的回归：[i] 在这个人命如草芥的世界，选择不杀人，本身就是一种反抗。 [/i]


[h3] 叙事：让玩家成为“不完美的自己” [/h3]

《赛博朋克2077》的叙事有一个被严重低估的优点：它允许你做一个“糟糕的人”。

你可以接任务然后放鸽子，NPC会打电话骂你；
你可以为了钱出卖朋友，然后看着他在酒吧里醉醺醺地诅咒你；
你可以在最终决战前，突然决定去赌场把所有钱输光 —— 游戏不会阻止你，只是会记录下这一切。

这种“不完美的自由”才是真正的角色扮演。不像某些游戏，给你一个“善良/邪恶”的二元选择，然后给你两套完全不同的剧情 —— 2077的善恶是模糊的，你的每个选择都会带来意想不到的后果，而这些后果往往不是“好”或“坏”能概括的。


[h3] 与《往日之影》：一次迟到的“补完” [/h3]

DLC《往日之影》不仅是新地图和新任务，[b] 它补全了本体缺失的灵魂 [/b]：

本体讲的是“如何在赛博世界活下去”，DLC讲的是“如何有尊严地死去”；
本体让你成为传奇，DLC让你明白传奇的代价；
本体给你选择，DLC告诉你 —— 有些选择，根本没有正确答案。

[spoiler] 尤其是新角色“所罗门·李德”，他不是一个传统意义上的“盟友”或“敌人”，而是一个[b] 被体制异化的理想主义者 [/b]。看着他为了所谓的“大局”一步步堕入深渊，你会问自己：如果我在他的位置，会做得更好吗？ [/spoiler]


[h3] 给这个“完美时代”的一记耳光 [/h3]

在2026年，我们习惯了AI生成的完美剧情、算法推荐的精准内容、社交媒体上的精致人设。而《赛博朋克2077》像一记耳光，告诉我们：
不完美才是真实的 —— 卡顿、诡异的物理引擎，反而让夜之城有了“活着的质感”；
选择没有正确答案 —— 游戏里每个任务都让你在“烂”和“更烂”之间做选择，就像现实生活；
反抗注定失败——就像强尼·银手炸掉荒坂塔，什么都没改变，但“反抗”这个行为本身，就是意义。

[h3] 最后，用游戏里一个无关紧要的细节来结束这篇测评： [/h3]

在夜之城某个偏僻的角落，有一个无人问津的自动售货机。如果你买一瓶可乐，它会用机械音说：“谢谢惠顾，祝你有美好的一天。” 但如果你连续买十瓶，它会说：“检测到重复购买行为。请确认您不是AI。请确认您还活着。”

这大概就是《赛博朋克2077》想告诉我们的：在这个万物皆可数据化的时代，唯一能证明你“还活着”的，就是那些不完美、不理性、不可预测的 —— [i] 人性。 [/i]

Cyberpunk 2077 is genuinely the best game I’ve ever played. Night City feels alive in a way no other open world does. Every street, building, and character has personality. The story is incredible, the soundtrack is perfect, and the characters actually make you care about them. Johnny Silverhand alone is more memorable than entire casts from other games.

What really makes the game special is the freedom. You can play as a stealth hacker, a cyber ninja, a walking tank, or some insane combination of all three. The combat feels smooth, the visuals are still some of the best in gaming, and the atmosphere is unmatched, especially at night with the neon lights and music blasting.

Yeah, the launch was rough, but after the updates and Phantom Liberty, this game became an absolute masterpiece. Most games are fun while you play them. Cyberpunk 2077 is a world you actually miss after you finish it.

Cyberpunk 2077 fue mucho más que un simple videojuego para mí. Fue una experiencia que me marcó de una manera que pocas obras han logrado. Cada historia, cada personaje, cada conversación y cada rincón de Night City me atraparon por completo. Disfruté cada segundo, cada minuto y cada hora que pasé dentro de ese mundo.

Entre todo lo que viví, Johnny Silverhand se convirtió en uno de los personajes más memorables que he conocido. Lo que comenzó como una relación complicada terminó convirtiéndose en algo mucho más profundo. Sus ideas, sus errores, sus sueños y la forma en que veía el mundo hicieron que cada conversación con él tuviera un peso especial. Ver cómo evolucionaba su relación con V fue una de las partes más emocionantes de toda la historia.

Cyberpunk me hizo reír, reflexionar, emocionarme y cuestionar muchas cosas. Es difícil explicar con palabras todo lo que me hizo sentir, porque fue algo que va más allá de jugar: fue vivir una aventura inolvidable junto a personajes que se sienten reales. Night City, Johnny, V y todas las historias que encontré en el camino dejaron una huella que difícilmente voy a olvidar. No fue solo un juego; fue una experiencia que siempre voy a recordar. 

Gracias, Night City.

А если серьёзно, после глобального патча 2.0 игру сильно переделали, решил пройти полностью и не ошибся. Игра затянула на несколько сотен часов на одном дыхании. После всех доработок, багов всё ещё хватает, но сейчас эта игра просто шедевр и явно входит в топ-5 лучших игр, в которые я когда-либо играл.
Так же однозначно рекомендую пройти с дополнением "Призрачная свобода", картина мира игры станет в разы шире, глубже и насыщенней. А сам Пёсий город удивляет своим изобилием контента в пределах одного стадиона.
Эта игра однозначно одна из тех, которую стоит пройти.
Launched broken, spent 3-4 years fixing it anyway — CDPR said "we're not done" and actually meant it. Respect. Play it.

Are there obejectively better games out there? Yes. Have I replayed them and enjoyed them as much as Cyberpunk? No, not even close. If you love dystopian sci-fi, a killer soundtrack, good characters, a good plot, and a fun combat system with lots of variation, then just play the dang game choom. I could critique it and point out where it's lacking, but it really is a solid game that deserves a playthrough or 5.

cyberpunk 2077 and its dlc are so good its eating up my laptop's resources.
玩不进去，操作手感太差，剧情过得太慢，角色动作慢说话也慢，加了很多花里胡哨的玩法但是纯耗时间，没有什么乐趣。超梦最后开热能找那个什么东西把我整破防了，不想玩了。

Fun game, good world to rampage through.  Still the odd bug where I can drive through buildings before they load if I'm going fast enough, probably due to pc not having the greatest specs.  All in all a great experience.

I waited a very long time to start playing, and i am insanely impressed. Gameplay and progression is awesome, the graphics are INSANE! Although you do need to do some research on graphics settings to make sure its dialed in. After playing couple hours I looked up some videos about the graphics and made some adjustments and my mind was completely blown away. It runs perfectly on pretty much any rig, you just got to optimize it to your pc specs. I'm running a rtx 3080 with 7800x3d and with medium-high settings (DLAA) I'm running 110 fps super smooth. but you can pretty much get any frames you want with tweaking the settings. I haven't dipped into the ray tracing just yet as i really like the DLAA settings, its super clear and very sharp. 

The progression could feel a little bit overwhelming as there's a bit of depth there with cyberware, but you will get the hang of it pretty quickly. The skill tree is also pretty straight forward.

Only thing that it lacks for me is gun customization.  Its a bit shallow and lacks the depth I would expect from a triple A rpg. Other than that at the moment I dont really have any other complaints. 

The main story has been awesome so far, but i have not finished it yet. Alot to do with the side quest, very immersive side activities. 

I'm super hooked right now and loving it! I would recommend this game 100%

So for a little bit of context, I did not like Cyberpunk at first cause my first impression of it was at launch on the PS4. I will forever regard this as my lesson to never pre-order anything ever again.

And with that out of the way, this game's pretty damn good now. Only real gripe I'd have with it is the combat feels a bit clunky no matter the playstyle (weapons kinda feel like they lack oomph) but it's not something too jarring.

The vibe of Night City really makes it feel like it's a living city with some people doing various weird/freaky things. The story is really memorable between some stupid little side objective and a high-stakes main mission, all with a side of allowing to approach it whatever way you want: wanna go full social engineering on your foes? Maybe sneaking by? Blowing their heads of with your mind? Or maybe just the good ol' big iron? The game really promotes getting creative with it and that's commendable. Weapons are mostly fun, especially the unique ones that can get a little wacky with their effects.

Safe for me to say, CDPR redeemed themselves. I'll be back for Phantom Liberty sometime (and the rumored second DLC).

Al principio era un sinsentido, porque tiene menús amplios de habilidades , o al menos confusos, pero en cuanto empece a levelear mis armas ufff papa todo cobró sentido , gracias a mi amigo GhostWolf que me regalo el juego , una verdadera joya

Based anime launched when they updated the game to be how it should have been at launch and this saved CP2077 and CDPR to an extent.

Games good enough but feels like it was almost going to be extremely progressive and just skimmed on not doing that which gives me fears for TW4, no anime will save that if it's DEI garbage.
Another fear for the future of this companies games is the fact CP2077 launched like hot steaming ♥♥♥♥, it was BAD, really bad, and they thought that was an acceptable way launch a title, especially since the hype and expectations were so high after TW3, which is still beloved today, shadey practices going on behind the scenes.

Character creations is good.
Graphics good.
Sounds good.
Story is okay
Side content is average, hit and miss.
Characters are average, very hit and miss.
World design is good.

All in all The story and characters should have been FAR better, the world building is lacking, however the graphics, sounds, terrain etc were good. Atmosphere was not bad.

TLDR: It's decent, bit cringe and some bits feel forced, but holds up okay, is it worth $124 AUD in 2026? Nah. Wait for a half price sale, pick it up then. 
Is it full of DEI stuff? Ehhhhhh it makes me worried about TW4 but it holds it off enough to enjoy the game.

ɪᴛᴀʟɪᴀɴ:
Cyberpunk 2077 è un gioco che non ha quasi bisogno di recensioni. È ormai un titolo imprescindibile. Al lancio è stato considerato uno dei giochi più buggati di sempre, ma nel tempo è diventato un vero capolavoro.

Non saprei nemmeno da dove iniziare a parlarne, e probabilmente non ce n'è bisogno. Godetevelo, fate le vostre scelte e vivete la vostra storia. Il gioco offre diversi finali e permette di costruire rapporti con i personaggi che incontrerete lungo il cammino.

Ricordate però una cosa: per quanto un corporativo possa aiutarvi, alla fine rimarrà sempre un vostro nemico. A Night City ognuno sopravvive come può e con i mezzi che ha.

E un ultimo consiglio: siete un mercenario. Non affezionatevi troppo alle persone che incontrate, o rischierete di ritrovarvi con il cuore in gola.
-
ᴇɴɢʟɪꜱʜ:
Cyberpunk 2077 is a game that barely needs a review. At this point, it's a must-have title. It was considered one of the buggiest game launches ever, but over time it evolved into an outstanding experience.

I wouldn't even know where to begin describing it, and honestly, I probably don't need to. Just play it, make your choices, and experience your own story. The game offers multiple endings and allows you to build meaningful relationships with many of its characters.

Just remember one thing: no matter how much a corpo helps you, they'll always be your enemy in the end. In Night City, everyone survives however they can.

And one last piece of advice: you're a mercenary. Don't get too attached to the people you meet, or you might end up with your heart in your throat.
Excelente juego, aunque ahora entiendo porque se quejan mucho de los bugs jaja

I hated this game when it first came out and it deserved the hate cause it simply didn't work. I played on my brother's PC and just hated the fact that something this well written was absolutely unplayable.
Even after 2.0 came out i refused to believe that this game has improved and had forgotten all about it. I got my new laptop and there was steam sale so i bought it on a whim.
I swear to god if  i had known this game was this amazing i would have bought it at full price much earlier.
CDPR outdid themselves. I cannot even fathom the scale of this game. 

The characters , music, combat and exploration is perfect. 
The main NPC's along with protagonists are some the best written characters i have seen so far.

I haven't even mentioned the lore. ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ they have so much of it. I don't think i have even scratched the surface of the overall lore of this world. 
I have done only one ending for both the base game and DLC.  I intend to do all of them.

And pls if you haven't watched Cyberpunk: Edgrunners anime then most definitely watch it.
Also listen to the game's music if possible.

This game is genuinely breathtaking.

The game is simply awasome if you are into massive openworld RPG. The direction and story this offers is unique and engaging. You will defenitly feel overwhelmed with the things to do and how to manage your character/ Which power to have. But it will sink in due time. This game is really special to me because of it's asthetics and vibe, I really loved and enjoyed playing it. After completing the story I got bored and stopped playing, but if you are an explorer type of guy then you are in for a best time.

Favorite game of all time now, such an open book. I came into this game expecting a satire, primary mission driven, lame slop game, was then given such a content-filled banger. 

I've grown to love V and Johnny and think that they have the potential to have the best character-dynamic (depending on how you decide to progress your relationship) of all time. With this, every character feels pretty well-written, as well as they can be with the amount of interactable npcs in the game. 

The combat is addicting, I feel so powerful playing the game now and can't wait to replay with an entirely different build. I feel inclined to progress my status in NC as V and become that legend he always strived to be, I have yet to finish the main campaign or Phantom Liberty, but they are both so so so amazing. The amount of side missions, long or not, along with the gigs and random encounters, it feels like there's never a lack of content. I don't know what I'm going to do when I finish the game 100%. 

I will say, though, after progging my build and getting all T5+ cyberware and a sandevistan (And all the phantom liberty bonus cyberware), I feel as though I'm far over-powered to even bosses. There's nothing that's given me a tough time thus far, and I haven't died naturally to an enemy since the very beginning. 

9.5/10

I wasn't an early adopter of this game so I cannot comment on the mess that was released at launch but oh boy, this game is fantastic! If you're a fan on cyberpunk themed films. tv and games such as Bladerunner, Altered carbon, ghost in a shell etc then you'll love this. So much inspiration oozes in this title and this is up there with one of the most immersive, living cities in gaming. Whether you want to rush through the story guns blazing or take a more methodical slow route, taking in the sights (Which I recommend) then this game will provide.  While not without fault or areas that can still be improved, it's  modern classic with tight gunplay, story and visuals that will impress on every level, regardless of the type of player you are.
first chapters are slow, but once you get into it, the story is great and the gameplay is addictive
Cyberpunk is hands down- one of the best video game experiences of all time. If you want pure immersion out of a game, play it.
Johnny Silverhand te quiero mucho. Vaya juego, los mejores personajes y misiones que he jugado y cada uno de los finales es una puta barbaridad
Love this game, the vibe, the stories, The city itself is amazing. Coming back for my 6th Playthrough. Had this on xbox one when it came out then xbox series s and the best so far on PC.
Одна из лучших игр, в которые я играл. Всем рекомендую в неё поиграть — она вам 100% понравится.

Termine de completar tanto la historia principal como el DLC Phantom Liberty y la verdad es un juegazo, uno de los mejores juegos de mundo abierto que he jugado. La historia principal es atrapante, los personajes están muy bien desarrollados y la ciudad de Night City se siente viva y llena de detalles. 
En cuanto al apartado gráfico se ve bastante bien y la banda sonora es increíble. Además las distintas formas de desarrollar al personaje te permiten jugar de maneras diferentes y hacen que te den ganas de volver a empezar una partida.

En resumen: tremenda obra maestra, 100/10 y GOD

Night City sets a new bar when it comes to level of detail and immersion in an open world game. Every inch of the landscape feels like it's been hand crafted. Quite impressive to say the least.

The gameplay is fun. There is a lot of different styles you can take with all the ability/combat trees.

The main story and voice acting is stellar as well. Side quests are hit or miss but everything is voice acted.

Tons of collectables and optional stuff to do.

Cyberpunk really shines if you invest some time into mods as well.

Despite all the positives its got going for it, Cyberpunk still does have some clunky issues and bugs. The game crashed occasionally but not so often that it hindered my gameplay too much. That being said I think the positives far outweigh the cons at this point. I heard it had a really rocky launch which is why I waited so long to try it. 

It's worth it to jump in now for sure.

CDPR managed to pull this out of the depths of bad corporate decisions (ironic, given the context of the game) and make one of the most immersive, fun, and thought-provoking gaming experiences I've ever had. I'll boot this one up every couple of years and it always remains fresh. Beautifully constructed near-future cyberpunk world that oozes charisma around every corner and dark alleyway. Also the soundtrack is SUPER punchy, I love it. And the lore? Awesome. My only gripe is that it takes a few hours to really get going (at least it was for me) and I initially struggled with getting used to it's various systems. But once it all started to click it was nonstop fun from there.

The DLC is 1000% worth it by the way. Seamlessly integrates with the rest of the game and is very high quality, almost moreso in some ways than the base game.

An entertaining and depressing glimpse into the future with a cast of very compelling characters and an immersive world that reflects a lot of the darkest aspects of our society taken to their logical extremes. The combat is satisfying, though can get pretty easy at times. This emotional rollercoaster of a game feels extremely prophetic and will remain relevant for decades to come

The city and world around are vast and detailed.  The driving is fun reminiscent of GTA.  The combat is a good first person shooter.  The stories complex and the characters involving, giving a emotion connection to them.

Однозначно играть. Стоит своих денег и стоит времени потраченного на прохождение сюжетных и побочных заданий. Интересные и проработанные главные персонажи. Если не пропускать диалоги, то можно насладиться пропитанной философскими нотками историей.  Очень красивая подача стиля киберпанка, как будто побывал в фильме Судья Дред. Cyberpunk 2077 - игра которая дарит эмоции.
except for a few bugs and the map being ♥♥♥♥♥♥ at times it is fun to play

Cyberpunk 2077 es uno de esos juegos que te dejan un vacío cuando terminan.

Llegué por la curiosidad de explorar Night City, pero me quedé por sus personajes, su historia y la increíble atmósfera que consigue transmitir en cada rincón de la ciudad. Pocas veces un videojuego me ha hecho sentir tan conectado con su mundo y con las personas que lo habitan.

Después de ver Cyberpunk: Edgerunners, volver a recorrer las calles de Night City tuvo un significado completamente diferente. Encontrar referencias, recordar momentos de la serie y sentir que ambas historias comparten el mismo espíritu hizo que la experiencia fuera aún más especial.

Este juego me regaló horas de diversión, momentos emocionantes, risas, tristeza y una banda sonora que todavía sigo escuchando. No es perfecto, pero para mí eso nunca importó. Lo que importa es que consiguió algo que muy pocos juegos logran: hacerme sentir que realmente estuve allí.

Gracias, Night City. Y recuerda: nunca dejes de luchar por tus sueños, choom. 🌙💛❤️

Main Game 5/5
Phantom Liberty 5/5
Achievements 5/5
Achievement Completion: 57/57 (100%)

Cyberpunk 2077 is one of the strangest gaming experiences I've had.

I bought the game at launch and, like many people, experienced some of the issues that surrounded the release. Despite that, I still gave it a fair chance and put around 11 hours into it before moving on. Some time later I came back and tried again, putting another 10 or so hours into a new save, but for whatever reason the game still wasn't clicking with me.

Then recently I decided to give it one more chance.

This time something changed.

Not only did the game finally click, but I couldn't stop playing it. I became so invested that after finishing the game I immediately started a second playthrough and completed the entire thing again while working towards 100% achievements.

Once you get a grasp of the mechanics, skill trees and general systems, everything starts to fall into place. The game does a lot and can feel overwhelming at first, but once it clicks it becomes incredibly rewarding.

The main story is outstanding.

The characters, writing, setting and atmosphere are some of the best I've experienced in gaming. Night City feels alive in a way very few open worlds manage to achieve. There is always something happening, whether it's a serious story moment, a side quest, random humour, or simply exploring the city itself.

The game also offers a huge amount of freedom in how you approach combat and progression. Different builds can completely change how the game feels, making multiple playthroughs genuinely worthwhile rather than simply replaying the same experience.

As a fan of Cyberpunk: Edgerunners, I also loved the references and connections throughout the game. They never felt forced and added another layer of enjoyment to an already fantastic world.

Phantom Liberty deserves special praise.

The expansion is exceptional from start to finish. The new area is fantastic, the story is brilliant and the new characters are some of the best in the entire game. Solomon Reed, played by Idris Elba, and Song So Mi quickly became two of my favourite characters in Cyberpunk 2077. Their performances and the choices surrounding them create some of the strongest moments in the entire game.

If you enjoy Cyberpunk 2077, Phantom Liberty is not optional in my opinion. It is a must-buy expansion and one of the best DLCs I have played.

Achievement-wise, I found the list very respectful of the player's time. There are a few achievements that benefit from planning ahead, multiple playthroughs or smart save points, but I never felt forced into repetitive grinding. During my first playthrough I did experience a few issues with achievements not unlocking correctly, which ultimately contributed to my second run, but thankfully I enjoyed the game enough that this never felt like a chore.

After 223 hours and 100% achievement completion, Cyberpunk 2077 isn't just one of my favourite games, it's my game of the decade.

It took several attempts to finally click with me, but once it did, I couldn't put it down.

a brutal story about a life in an unforgiving city. Stuck in the middle of decisions where you know someone is going to fall, even that someone may or may not be yourself. 

A world that reminds you to live in the moment and enjoy those around you in the now - because tomorrow is never a given for you or for anyone else. A journey where you chase the dream of becoming a famous legend and realize over time you just want to enjoy the quiet parts of life.

Cherish your chooms. 

Now onto the game -

loved everything about this game besides the nitpicking of wishing more of the world felt alive and interactive - Crimson Desert has ruined me for this. But this game will be timeless, its a world you can truly get lost in - I just wish there was like an "endless" mode after you complete the game or a "new game +" to complete everything you want. It is the one drawback of having a narrative driven game in what feels like a "lived in" organic world that you just want to exist in.  Cuz man it is absolutely gorgeous.

After finishing it and realizing i wish i did more, the game doesnt really provide a way to continue. But in a way, it makes sense... because that is how life is. You experience what you can in Night City, and when its done, you look back on how you experienced it. I loved every minute of it.

I think the best thing about Cyberpunk is that it is not repetitive, making it very hard to get bored of it.

The music in this game is so good that it makes you wish Samurai were a real band

Breve y al grano: El juego es una joyita de inicio a fin. Y listo. 

El problema quizá que veo es que tiene una NARRATIVA algo densa al inicio y aunque no es difícil de entender el lore con el paso de las horas si que premia detenerse un rato y leer, escuchar o no saltar diálogos, esto es VITAL para no perderse. 

El juego habla de muchos temas en el modo historia y aunque a mi personalmente me gustó mucho, para varios jugadores puede ser absurdamente denso en su forma de abordar temas. De nuevo, si la historia muy detallada o entrelazada no es de tu agrado quizá en este caso no te parezca tan llamativo. Pero claro, esperá que te digo qué tal es en gameplay.

En el aspecto TÉCNICO es increíble; absolutamente nada que ver a como salió, ahora está absurdamente bien optimizado si llegas a los requisitos mínimos y aún en configuraciones básicas se ve bastante bien pulido. Medio patina a veces el motor con uno que otro error gráfico pero es esporádico.

Como juego y experiencia lúdica sin duda es un salto hacia adelante en comparación con otros títulos de mundo abierto, mucho mejor lograda la sensación de progreso, las armas, las habilidades y mejoras corporales le dan muchas combinaciones chulas, además que las influencias de juego RPG, aunque modestas, suman lo justo para hacer el juego dinámico y disfrutable de probar. En este caso debo hacer énfasis: El juego está BRUTAL en el aspecto lúdico, es simplemente entretenido aún si no prestas mucha atención a la historia, es disfrutable, tanto o más que un GTA en modo historia (al menos los que van a fecha) aunque, claro, en narrativa o historia claramente Cyberpunk quiere dar más densidad al mundo, más detalle y GTA que es evidente, tiene historias más simples pero efectivas y resumibles en un párrafo corto.  En ese sentido Cyberpunk hace un trabajo excelente siendo un producto único con personalidad fuerte y gran carisma. 

Cyberpunk es un juego increíble, muy entretenido que ofrece muchísimas horas de combinaciones creativas, diversión e inmersión en un mundo futurista. 

Sin duda recomendadísimo en 2026!

I love FPS games. I love story-driven single player games. I love RPGs. I am a fan of the Witcher universe as I read all the books and enjoyed all 3 Witcher games by CDPR. AND YET, I couldn't get into Cyberpunk 2077 for 4 years - 
I owned the game since its launch and tried starting the playthrough multiple times with no success. Maybe it was a bit of arcade-ish feeling of the gameplay, maybe it was the cyberpunk setting I didn't care about previously or maybe it was too much neon and pukey transition screens with too much brightness and colors... 

But I finally got to have a real playthrough in 2026 and for the last couple of months I finally got to thoroughly enjoy this game and I am happy I gave it a chance. It is definitely a great game with a fun gameplay loop and unique, yet comprehensively immersive setting. What's more important is that game has that Witcher 3 magic touch when it comes to quest design and the feeling of immersion. There are a lot of great and fun things that so many people other than myself told everyone about in these reviews during these past 4 years. 

Long story shorter - the game is fire and it is well-polished (pun intended) by now. I do recommend playing with the Phantom Liberty DLC as it adds a great deal to the gameplay and lore of the main game. So, yeah... Just one more gig, choom, and we'll make it to the top in the Night City!

Cyberpunk 2077 is one of the best games that I’ve played. It had a rough start back in 2020, but they put the time and effort into fixing it up, and it is now one of the greatest story games I’ve ever played.

The story is massive and full of options, with multiple endings and dialogue choices. It’s an amazing game for people who like having choices that affect their experience.

The gameplay is also great. The gunplay feels really solid, and the same goes for the melee weapons. It never felt like a cheap game. Hacking while fighting also felt very fluid and easy, creating some really cool and cinematic moments.

The characters are well written. You genuinely feel for them and feel like you have real connections with them.

The graphics are some of the best out there, especially with ray tracing and path tracing. The soundtrack in this game is amazing. I’ve even saved a couple of tracks to my Spotify playlist.

The performance of the game varies depending on what you play it on, and it can be a bit demanding. For me, my PC ran it insanely well at 4K and 120 FPS.

The DLC is 100% worth purchasing.

Overall, this game is a 10/10 masterpiece, and it’s one of those games that I can always replay.

Voice acting is very good, Keanu Reeves is excellent as Johnny Silverhand (and I am sure that Idris Elba is excellent in the expansion but, sadly, he is tied to the least appealing storyline in the game, which I abandoned as soon as I could).

All in all I had great time in Night City.

No jogo Cyberpunk 2077, acompanhamos a jornada de V, um mercenário que vê sua vida mudar completamente após um trabalho teoricamente simples dar errado.

Ao longo da jornada, conhecemos personagens marcantes como Johnny Silverhand, Panam Palmer, Judy Alvarez, Takemura, Rogue e, seu melhor amigo Jackie Welles. Cada um com histórias e personalidades diferentes que ajudam a tornar a narrativa principal mais interessante.

A ambientação de Night City é muito bonita e detalhada, a história é interessante e os personagens conseguem prender a atenção. O jogo oferece bastante liberdade para montar seu personagem e escolher diferentes formas de jogar, tanto na forma de abordar missões quanto nos relacionamentos e decisões tomadas durante a campanha, o que deixa a experiência mais pessoal e imersiva. 

Minha experiência foi bastante positiva, recomendo!

Playing this game was a once-in-a-lifetime experience. The visuals are beautiful, the music and audio design are masterfully done, and the voice acting is top-notch across the board. The combat arsenal has incredible diversity and depth, enough to spend hours optimizing your build. The story, particularly the Phantom Liberty expansion, is an absolute cinematic masterpiece. I love how Night City constantly challenges the player's morality and many choices force you to simply pick the lesser evil, perfectly showcasing the grim nature of the city. Overall, it's an amazing, immersive experience that I will definitely replay a couple more times.

That being said, the game does have its flaws:

- Bugs: While it's obviously nowhere near as bad as launch day, the game still suffers from annoying visual and gameplay bugs. For example, during the Sasquatch boss fight, a bug caused her to stand completely still for the rest of the fight after dropping her hammer. Even reloading the game multiple times didn't fix that, ruining the fight for me.

- Underutilized Enemy Design: Despite the depth of V’s arsenal, the enemies aren't challenging or creative enough to make you utilize that diversity. Most enemies, including the bosses, ultimately just feel like bullet sponges or punching bags.

- Pacing and Cohesion: The main storyline lacks tight cohesion. While individual parts are excellent (like Evelyn’s subplot), the overall pacing could be better. Characters are often introduced and thrown away too quickly, and certain plot points feel like a compilation of unfortunate events slapped together. The game expects them to hit hard emotionally, but the player hasn't had enough context or time to truly connect with them. Johnny’s arc is a prime example: after the "Chippin' In" questline, V and Johnny develop mutual respect for each other. Yet, right after talking to a certain someone at Embers, Johnny seemingly disregards that heart-to-heart and impulsively just asks V to give him control, lacking any real interest in what V actually wants, even if you account for the Relic's degradation forcing his hand.

Despite these flaws, when you look at the game as a whole, it is a fantastic achievement that gave me an experience very few games ever have. I highly recommend it.

第一次在游戏中感受到足够真实的恋爱体验，也是第一次在游戏中感受到如此沉重压抑的氛围。
对于有轻微强迫症的全收集爱好者来说，打通2077的每一个结局都需要我花很长一段时间去消化，以至于断断续续玩了快半年才彻底封盘，甚至过了两周才写下这段文字。
去年我在想，如果资本主义物化人类的尽头真的存在，那大概就是2077中所描绘的场景。但经历年初小红书的赛博对账之后，我发现这一切居然都多多少少的已经在世界的某个角落上演。原来，人真的无法想象自己从未见过的东西。
说实话，这破游戏罐头任务一堆，载具手感极差，身边总是跟着一个满嘴跑火车的家伙，画面光怪陆离，光污染拉满。但后劲可比前几年看《赛博朋克：边缘行者》大多了。哪怕看着V不断被背叛，一步步走向既定的悲剧结局，还是不断吸引着我打开，仿佛一个苦行僧还没有走完属于他的旅程。
不管V怎么努力，这么多个结局中竟然找不到一个给玩家带来希望，全是背叛和算计、失望和遗憾。当失去所有朋友、爱人，无法再使用义体的V走在夜之城街头，如同废人般的他其实已经死了。正如游戏中的一句台词：

「夜之城没有活着的传说」

After 50+ hours, I genuinely think Cyberpunk 2077 is one of the best open-world RPGs I've ever played, and honestly, it might be my favorite story-driven game period.

What surprised me the most wasn't the graphics, even though they're absolutely insane. Night City is one of the few game worlds that actually feels alive. During the day, the city is packed with people going about their lives, and at night the neon lights make everything look straight out of a sci-fi movie. Sometimes I'd find myself driving around with no objective just because I wanted to take in the atmosphere. There are very few games where simply existing in the world is enjoyable, but Cyberpunk nails it.

The story is what really sold me, though. I expected a good story, but I didn't expect to become so invested in the characters. The game somehow makes you care about the people you meet, their struggles, and the choices you make throughout the journey. There were multiple moments where I sat staring at my screen after a mission ended just thinking about what happened. The writing feels mature, emotional, and surprisingly human for a game set in a futuristic dystopia.

What I love most is that Cyberpunk isn't afraid to teach difficult lessons. Throughout the game, you're constantly reminded that life isn't fair, that power isn't always enough, and that sometimes there simply isn't a perfect ending waiting for you. Night City doesn't bend to your will just because you're the protagonist. In fact, one of the biggest themes of the game is realizing how small you can be compared to the forces controlling the world around you.

The Peralez storyline is probably the best example of this. By that point, V can become an absolute monster capable of taking down entire gangs, elite soldiers, and some of the most dangerous people in Night City. Yet when you uncover what's happening to Jefferson and Elizabeth Peralez, you're suddenly faced with something far beyond guns, cyberware, or brute force. You start pulling on a thread that leads to a conspiracy so massive and unsettling that you realize you're completely out of your depth. Then there's Mr. Blue Eyes, a character who appears briefly yet somehow manages to leave one of the biggest impressions in the entire game. The mystery surrounding him and what he represents creates this feeling that there are powers operating in the shadows that even legends like V can't truly understand. It was one of the few moments in gaming where I genuinely felt powerless, and that's exactly why it was so memorable.

The game constantly delivers moments like that. It explores identity, mortality, ambition, friendship, sacrifice, and what it really means to leave a mark on the world. It asks questions that don't always have easy answers, and that's what makes the story stick with you long after you've finished playing.

The side quests deserve a special mention because they don't feel like filler. In a lot of open-world games, side content feels like a checklist. In Cyberpunk, some side quests are so well-written that they feel like main story missions. Many of them introduce memorable characters and stories that stuck with me long after I completed them. Some of the most emotional moments I experienced weren't even part of the main story.

The lore is also incredible. The deeper you dig into Night City, the more fascinating it becomes. The corporations, gangs, politics, technology, and history all blend together to create a world that feels believable despite how futuristic it is. You can spend hours learning about the world without ever touching the main story, and you'll still find yourself discovering new details.

The freedom you have is another huge plus. Whether you're talking your way through situations, hacking everything in sight, sneaking around enemies, or charging in guns blazing, the game lets you create your own version of V. The cyberware system is one of my favorite progression systems in any RPG because every upgrade genuinely changes how you approach the game.

Now, the game isn't perfect. I've had more crashes than I would've liked, and I've run into bugs that occasionally break immersion. Sometimes NPCs behave strangely, and there are moments where the game still shows some technical roughness. But honestly, those issues never outweighed everything the game does right.

Cyberpunk 2077 is one of those rare games that completely pulls you in. The combination of incredible visuals, unforgettable characters, emotional storytelling, deep lore, meaningful choices, amazing music, and thought-provoking themes made it incredibly hard to put down. There were times when I wasn't just playing the game—I felt like I was living in Night City.

For all its flaws, Cyberpunk 2077 gave me something very few games ever have: a world I couldn't stop thinking about even when I wasn't playing. The fact that a game can entertain you is impressive. The fact that it can make you reflect on life, power, identity, and the things beyond your control is something special.

I'll just go out and say it - this might be one of the best games ever, and definitely one of the best I have ever played.

Cyberpunk 2077 simply pulls you in like very few other games could. Night city feels incredibly immersive, in large thanks to Mike Pondsmith's RPG series upon which it is based, but also thanks to the masterful game design and plot. CDprojekt took a page from the great Half-Life series as there are no cutscenes in a classical sense throughout the game. You almost never see the world outside V's eyes, and that just lets you inhabit their body on a level I can confidently say is unbeatable. You don't [i] play as [/i] V, you [i] are [/i] V. Even though a great majority of dialogue options don't have particularly far reaching consequences, they offer just enough variety to truly express yourself and give V your own spin.

But immersion means nothing if the story you immerse yourself into is as bland as real life, which this is categorically not. The plot hooks are numerous and engaging with an ensemble of characters so fleshed out you begin to question game from reality. Johnny especially is such a charismatic and strong persona many would get up and follow him to Arasaka tower if it existed today. He is probably Keanu Reeve's best performance. Romance character quests are also captivating: some better, some worse, some gutwrenching and some heartwarming, but that's just how life is. Don't expect too much from your date after their personal plotline is finished, though. You get a couple fun text interactions and that's about it.

But what about gameplay? The gunplay? Pristine. The options? Numerous. Do you want to sneak around like a ninja: deadly and silent? Sure, spec into reflexes, swap some chrome and be invisible. Do you want to go in guns blazing, faster than bullets and indestructible? Of course, grab a sandy and armor up like hell. Is standing back as your enemies drop like flies from the viruses you inject into their minds more your speed? Become a netrunner. There is so so many strategies, builds, fun little quirks one can explore that no two experiences can ever be the same. It's all so incredibly satisfying but at the same time full of expression that you just have to experience it to understand.

This is where we come to the few gripes I have about the game. Number one is the bugs. If [i]this[/i] is the fixed version of the game then day one must have been pandemonium. Cars dropping from the sky, sliding through the floor, npc's getting stuck, objectives not finishing, graphical glitches and at one time a flat out crash are just some of the wonders I personally experienced. Also car handling on PC is ... bad. I imagine some of it might be better on a game pad, but most of the cars in the game are flat out impossible to control with a keyboard. They seem to drift out more at lower speeds somehow, to the point making a 90 degree turn without crashing borders on impossible (maybe a bit exaggerated but does not feel great). Bikes are slightly better but still not perfect. These mistakes are however, in the grand scheme of things, pretty insignificant.

Overall a must play.

I believe i originally preordered this game way back in 2018, before it was delayed multiple times. By the time it came out, i wasn't even too broken up over the rough state the game was in. After years of waiting, the money i had spent on it hardly felt like it had ever existed. I played the game for 10 hours and then dropped it.

Fast Forward to 2026 and i get the urge to come give it another shot. I remembered enjoying the bit of the story I did get to experience, and i knew that the gameplay and bugginess of the game had been improved to various extents.

Having now binged nearly all of the content in the base game over the course of two weeks, i can confidently say that i am satisfied with my purchase of this game eight years ago. 
While occasionally rough around the edges, i did thoroughly enjoy the combat and character progression of the game. Running mostly a blade build let me enjoy an up close, frenetic combat style that i imagine to be wholly different but equally as enjoyable as to what might be experienced from focusing more on quickhacks or on smart weapons.
I found myself doing nearly all of the side jobs the game had to offer (save for the gigs, but i occasionally still did those if i stumbled into them), and was well satisfied with more of them than i would have expected. This is really all thanks to the character writing in this game. 

By far, the standout feature of Cyberpunk is its writing. Dialogue between characters feels natural and animations in important moments are incredibly well done. I found myself really invested in the relationships between many of the main cast, and especially the relationships that V has with Johnny, Panam and Judy. 
I won't speak on the endings too much, but i think they are all very well written. Something tells me that people were not happy with some of them when the game came out, but maybe i'm just being harsh on the average gamer in 2020.

I will say, the performance in the open world can sometimes be lacking, although i will admit that i run the game on ultra settings with a 2k monitor and average above 60 fps. I will especially mention that I never had any performance hiccups during important story scenes, I never once found myself taken out of a moment because of lag. The game also has occasional small bugs, like a cop walking around inside the sidewalk or a civilian loudly and repeatedly running their car into a wall 20 feet away from a conversation you're having. Nothing serious.

Honestly, my biggest complaint with the game is the content surrounding the romantic relationships in the game, or the lack of it. [spoiler] I can only speak on the relationship with Judy, but i imagine the others are quite the same. There's a whole big mission (and honestly a decent amount of setup in the background throughout the game) of this potential relationship blossoming, and once it finally does, the only thing that comes of it is occasional text conversations that eventually dry up, and a repeatable date mission in which you have access to a few small events and a set of dialogue that never changes and is largely the same as what you would get when you called Judy before you were even in a relationship. [/spoiler]
I think it speaks greatly to this game's strengths that this is my biggest issue with it.

I know this game has largely positive reviews today, but i imagine there are still a lot of people who view the game in the light it was (often justly) received in at launch (although there were certainly unrealistic expectations surrounding the game at the time as well). I hope any of those people who may have dropped the game like i did come give it another shot some time, it's really worth it.

I think I'd give this game a 9/10. I'm very happy I came back to it. Really looking forward to the DLC next time it goes on sale :)
